wines.name: Vega Sicilia Unico
wines.oak_regime: Aged a minimum of 10 years including time in large oak vats, American oak barrels and French barriques before bottle ageing

taste.summary: A Tinto Fino-led blend aged for a decade or more, building complexity of dried fruit, leather and cedar with a mineral, persistent finish.

pairings.why: Iberian roast pork's caramelised fat and delicate meat are lifted by the wine's dried-fruit depth and mineral backbone.
Crystalline sheep's milk salt amplifies the wine's cedar and tobacco notes.
Castilian slow-cooked lamb finds a structural equal in the wine's firm tannins and earthy complexity.

history.summary: Unico has been released since the early 20th century from the estate founded in 1864. Each vintage is released only when deemed ready by the winery, sometimes a decade or more after harvest.

milestones.event: Vega Sicilia estate founded in Valbuena de Duero
First documented Unico vintage
DO Ribera del Duero granted; Vega Sicilia among founding estates

wines.description: Vega Sicilia's flagship Tinto Fino blend, aged a minimum of ten years before release, from a Valbuena de Duero estate founded in 1864.

wines.name: Vega Sicilia Unico Reserva Especial
wines.oak_regime: Blend of three or more Unico vintages, aged in oak and bottle; released as a non-vintage blend

taste.summary: A non-vintage multi-year blend of Unico vintages, offering seamless integration of dried fruit, cedar and mineral notes across selected exceptional years.

pairings.why: The wine's dried-herb and spice register pairs naturally with herbed Castilian lamb.
Silky cured fat and nutty complexity complement the wine's tobacco and cedar tones.
Earthy mushroom depth echoes the wine's forest floor and mineral core.

history.summary: First released in 1986 as a blend of the 1970, 1968 and 1962 vintages, the Reserva Especial is assembled from selected Unico vintages to create a non-vintage wine of exceptional consistency. Each release carries three vintage years on the label.

milestones.event: First Reserva Especial released as a blend of 1970, 1968 and 1962 vintages
DO Ribera del Duero granted

wines.description: A non-vintage blend of selected Unico vintages released by Vega Sicilia to showcase outstanding years. Each edition is identified by the vintage years blended rather than a single harvest date.

wines.name: Valbuena 5 Grado
wines.oak_regime: Five years of ageing in American and French oak casks before release

taste.summary: Vega Sicilia's earlier-release sibling to Unico, aged five years in oak, with a more approachable but still deeply structured profile of dark cherry, cedar and mineral complexity.

pairings.why: Classic Castilian pairing; the wine's structure complements the richness of the roast.
Game's iron richness is balanced by the wine's firm tannic frame.
Nutty, crystalline cheese pulls out the wine's fruit and cedar notes.

history.summary: Named for its five years of oak ageing, Valbuena was historically produced alongside Unico as the shorter-aged wine of the estate. It was released as Valbuena 5 Grado to distinguish its ageing regime from the decade-plus Unico.

milestones.event: Vega Sicilia estate founded
DO Ribera del Duero granted

wines.description: Vega Sicilia's five-year-aged complement to Unico, blending Tinto Fino and Merlot into a structured, complex red that offers an earlier window into the estate's style.

wines.name: Alion Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in new French oak barriques

taste.summary: A modern, Bordeaux-influenced 100% Tinto Fino Reserva from Vega Sicilia's sister estate, with ripe black fruit, new French oak structure and a clean mineral finish.

pairings.why: The wine's firm tannins and black fruit are an ideal match for charred beef.
Tender Castilian lamb and the wine's cedar and fruit complexity are natural partners.
Firm sheep's milk cheeses mirror the wine's full structure and spice.

history.summary: Founded in 1991 as a separate estate project within the Vega Sicilia group, Alion was conceived to produce a modern, single-variety Tinto Fino wine aged in French barriques rather than the traditional oak regime of Unico. It brought a different stylistic approach to the group's portfolio.

milestones.event: Bodegas Alion founded as the second estate of the Vega Sicilia group
First vintage of Alion Reserva produced

wines.description: 100% Tinto Fino Reserva from the Vega Sicilia group's second estate, founded in 1991. Aged 14 months in new French oak, it presents a modern, structured counterpart to the classical Unico style.

wines.name: Pingus
wines.oak_regime: Aged approximately 18 months in new French oak barriques

taste.summary: A micro-production Tinto Fino from single old-vine parcels in La Horra, characterised by intense concentration of dark fruit, mineral depth and dense, ripe tannins that reward long cellaring.

pairings.why: The wine's concentration and dense tannins call for equally rich, fatty pork.
Collagen-rich braised beef underscores the wine's plum and licorice complexity.

pairings.dish: Aged Torta del Casar or Manchego Viejo
pairings.why: Bold, aged Spanish cheeses match the wine's power and minerality.

history.summary: Created by Danish winemaker Peter Sisseck from a tiny plot of old Tinto Fino vines in La Horra, the 1995 debut vintage of Pingus immediately attracted critical attention. Produced from biodynamically farmed, pre-phylloxera ungrafted vines, the wine is made in tiny quantities and distributed globally on allocation.

milestones.event: First vintage of Pingus produced from an old-vine parcel in La Horra
Dominio de Pingus estate formally established by Peter Sisseck

wines.description: Peter Sisseck's flagship micro-cuvee from pre-phylloxera Tinto Fino vines in La Horra. First made in 1995, Pingus is produced in tiny quantities and aged in new French oak for a wine of extreme concentration and mineral depth.

wines.name: Flor de Pingus
wines.oak_regime: Approximately 12 months in French oak barriques, proportion new

taste.summary: The second wine of Dominio de Pingus, produced from purchased fruit across the DO, showing similar mineral depth and concentration to Pingus but on a more accessible scale.

pairings.why: Castilian charcoal-grilled lamb pairs with the wine's fruit concentration and firm tannins.
The wine's earthy mineral core complements the nutty sweetness of Iberian pork.
Earthy umami from mushrooms echoes the wine's earthy, spice register.

history.summary: First produced in 1996 from purchased old-vine Tinto Fino fruit across Ribera del Duero, Flor de Pingus was initially conceived as the second wine of the estate but has developed a significant following in its own right, sharing the biodynamic philosophy of the estate.

milestones.event: First vintage of Flor de Pingus produced

wines.description: The second label of Dominio de Pingus, produced from purchased old-vine Tinto Fino fruit across the DO since 1996. It shares the estate's biodynamic ethos and delivers concentrated dark fruit with mineral depth.

wines.name: PSI
wines.oak_regime: Aged in used French oak barrels to preserve freshness

taste.summary: A fruit-forward, fresh expression of Tinto Fino from small grower parcels across the DO, with higher acidity and lighter oak influence than Flor de Pingus.

pairings.why: The wine's fresh acidity and lighter body work well with simple roasted poultry.
Red fruit and earthy notes complement the spiced pork.
The lighter style is versatile enough for rustic Castilian legume dishes.

history.summary: PSI is a collaborative project by Peter Sisseck with small family growers across Ribera del Duero, who farm their own old vines under guidance from the Pingus team. Launched with the 2007 vintage, it offers a more accessible introduction to the Sisseck philosophy.

milestones.event: PSI first vintage produced in collaboration with small growers

wines.description: A collaborative project between Peter Sisseck and small family growers across the DO, launched in 2007. PSI is the most accessible label from the Pingus estate, showing fresh red fruit and lighter oak with high acidity.

wines.name: Tinto Pesquera Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in American oak barrels

pairings.why: The wine's red fruit and oak spice complement traditional Castilian slow-roasted lamb.
Paprika-spiced sausage and the wine's cherry and vanilla notes are a classic match.
Firm sheep's milk cheese brings out the wine's fruit and vanilla character.

history.summary: Alejandro Fernandez planted his first commercial vines in 1972 and released his first Pesquera Crianza in 1975, at a time when Ribera del Duero was largely unknown outside Castile. His wines attracted international attention and were instrumental in establishing the DO in 1982.

milestones.event: Alejandro Fernandez plants first Tinto Fino vines in Pesquera de Duero
First Tinto Pesquera Crianza released
DO Ribera del Duero officially granted

wines.description: The entry-level Crianza from Alejandro Fernandez's Pesquera estate, the wine that helped establish DO Ribera del Duero's global reputation in the 1980s. Cherry fruit with American oak vanilla and Castilian character.

wines.name: Tinto Pesquera Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 18-24 months in American and French oak barrels

pairings.why: Castilian cochinillo's fat-rich meat works perfectly with the wine's structure and fruit depth.
Herb-coated lamb and the wine's cedar and tobacco notes reinforce each other.
Firm cheese amplifies the wine's dark fruit and earthy finish.

history.summary: Tinto Pesquera Reserva has been produced since the estate's early commercial years, adding additional oak ageing to create a more complex and age-worthy expression from Fernandez's Pesquera de Duero vineyards.

milestones.event: Estate planting begins in Pesquera de Duero
DO Ribera del Duero granted

wines.description: Tinto Pesquera Reserva from Alejandro Fernandez's estate in Pesquera de Duero, with extended oak ageing building leather and spice complexity on a base of dark Tinto Fino fruit.

wines.name: Tinto Pesquera Gran Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Minimum 24 months in American oak followed by extended bottle ageing at the winery

pairings.why: The wine's dried fruit and tobacco tones work with the gelatinous richness of braised beef.
Earthy intensity of game and mushroom matches the wine's mature complexity.
Intense, pungent cheese meets the wine's tobacco and leather depth.

history.summary: Tinto Pesquera Gran Reserva is only produced in exceptional vintages and represents the summit of the DO for Alejandro Fernandez's estate, combining the longest oak ageing with extended cellar time at the winery before release.

milestones.event: DO Ribera del Duero granted; first Pesquera Gran Reserva produced

wines.description: Produced only in top vintages, Tinto Pesquera Gran Reserva is the pinnacle of Alejandro Fernandez's estate range, with extended American oak ageing and complex dried fruit, leather and tobacco character.

wines.name: Pesquera Janus Gran Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Extended oak ageing over multiple years in American oak barrels, followed by long bottle ageing

pairings.why: The Castilian tradition of whole-roasted lamb and aged Ribera del Duero is a regional standard-bearer.
Gamey richness and truffle earth resonate with the wine's dried-fruit depth.
Long-aged cheese brings crystalline salt to mirror the wine's concentrated complexity.

history.summary: The Janus label commemorates the two-faced Roman god, representing the estate's dual identity as both innovator and guardian of tradition. Released only in the finest vintages, it represents years of extra ageing beyond the Gran Reserva and is distributed in very limited quantities.

milestones.event: First Janus Gran Reserva produced from the inaugural DO Ribera del Duero vintage

wines.description: Tinto Pesquera's rare prestige label, produced only in exceptional years and aged far beyond the Gran Reserva requirement. A collector's wine of concentrated dried fruit, tobacco and leather with extraordinary longevity.

wines.name: Aalto
wines.oak_regime: 15 months in French oak barriques, 80% new

pairings.why: The wine's ripe fruit and firm tannins stand up to the richness and aromatics of Castilian roast lamb.
Dark fruit and cedar complement charred beef fat and protein.
Firm Spanish sheep's milk cheeses harmonise with the wine's structure and fruit depth.

history.summary: Founded in 1999 by Mariano García, the former longtime winemaker of Vega Sicilia, together with Javier Zaccagnini. Aalto sources fruit from old Tinto Fino vines across several villages and was designed from the outset to showcase French-oak refinement on a Ribera del Duero platform.

milestones.event: Bodegas Aalto founded by Mariano García and Javier Zaccagnini; first vintage produced

wines.description: The flagship wine of Bodegas Aalto, founded in 1999 by former Vega Sicilia winemaker Mariano García. 100% Tinto Fino aged 15 months in French oak, with ripe dark fruit, precise acidity and elegant structure.

verified.address_quoted: Paraje Vallejo de Carril s/n, Quintanilla de Arriba, Valladolid 47360

wines.name: Aalto PS
wines.oak_regime: 20 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: The wine's power and complexity demand rich, traditional Castilian roasted meats.
Game's earthy intensity is a natural match for the wine's mineral and dark fruit character.
Dense cheese and sweet quince provide textural and flavour balance to the wine's intensity.

history.summary: Aalto PS (Pagos Seleccionados) is the estate's prestige single-vineyard level wine, produced from the oldest and most carefully selected Tinto Fino plots across several sub-zones. It was introduced alongside the Aalto flagship from the estate's first vintage.

milestones.event: Bodegas Aalto founded; Aalto PS first produced from selected old-vine plots

wines.description: Aalto PS (Pagos Seleccionados) is the prestige single-plot cuvee from Bodegas Aalto, sourced from the oldest Tinto Fino vines of the estate and aged 20 months in new French oak. Greater intensity and ageing potential than the flagship Aalto.

verified.address_quoted: Paraje Vallejo de Carril s/n, Quintanilla de Arriba, Valladolid 47360

wines.name: Mauro Tinto
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in French and American oak barrels

pairings.why: The wine's southern warmth and herb notes pair with charcoal-grilled lamb.
Tinto Fino's fruit and the spice of Garnacha and Syrah work with herbed pork.
Earthy, mineral notes in the wine echo truffle and woodland mushroom complexity.

history.summary: Mariano García founded Mauro in 1980 at Tudela del Duero while still working at Vega Sicilia. The vineyards straddle the DO Ribera del Duero boundary, so wines are classified as VT Castilla y León. Mauro was one of the first non-DO wines from the region to gain serious critical recognition.

milestones.event: Bodegas Mauro founded by Mariano García at Tudela del Duero
First Mauro vintage produced; classified as VT Castilla y León

wines.description: Mariano García's flagship VT Castilla y León blend from vineyards at Tudela del Duero, outside the DO boundary. A complex marriage of Tinto Fino, Garnacha and Syrah aged in French and American oak.

verified.address_quoted: Carretera de Villabáñez, Km 1, 47320 Tudela del Duero (Valladolid)

wines.name: Mauro VS
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: Concentrated fruit and dense structure call for the richest, most celebratory Castilian roasts.
Game intensity and the wine's dark, mineral complexity are a natural fit.
Pure truffle aromatics parallel the wine's earthy, mineral notes.

history.summary: Mauro VS (Vendimia Seleccionada, or Selected Harvest) is the prestige wine of the estate, produced from the oldest and most exceptional vine plots each vintage. First made in 1994, it is typically released several years after the flagship Mauro.

milestones.event: First vintage of Mauro VS from selected old-vine Tinto Fino and Garnacha parcels

wines.description: Mauro VS (Vendimia Seleccionada) is the prestige single-selection cuvee of Bodegas Mauro, produced only in top vintages from the oldest vine plots. Classified VT Castilla y León, it offers extraordinary concentration and ageing potential.

verified.address_quoted: Carretera de Villabáñez, Km 1, 47320 Tudela del Duero (Valladolid)

wines.name: Picaro del Aguila Tinto
wines.oak_regime: Aged in used French oak foudres and barrels for approximately 10 months

pairings.why: Fresh acidity and earthy fruit match the simplicity and richness of charcoal-grilled lamb.
The wine's lighter body and high acidity pair naturally with herb-roasted poultry.
Semi-cured sheep's milk cheese harmonises with the wine's red fruit and mineral notes.

history.summary: Pícaro del Águila was introduced alongside the estate's founding in 2010 as a more accessible entry into Jorge Monzon's certified-organic-farmed portfolio at La Aguilera. It uses a blend of varieties from the old mixed-planting parcels.

milestones.event: Dominio del Aguila founded; Picaro del Aguila Tinto first produced

wines.description: The entry-level red from Jorge Monzon's certified-organic estate at La Aguilera in Burgos, blending Tinto Fino, Garnacha and Albillo Mayor from old mixed-planting parcels.

wines.name: Dominio del Aguila Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in large French oak foudres

pairings.why: Classic Castilian lamb and the wine's dark fruit and mineral notes are a natural pairing.
Earthy mushroom and pork match the wine's herb and mineral register.
Firm sheep's milk cheese and the wine's mineral acidity create balance.

history.summary: Jorge Monzon founded Dominio del Aguila in 2010 with the aim of farming organically and producing wines of terroir transparency from old Tinto Fino vines at high altitude in La Aguilera, Burgos. The estate achieved certified-organic status from the Consejo Ecologico de Castilla y Leon.

milestones.event: Dominio del Aguila estate founded by Jorge Monzon; certified-organic status obtained from Consejo Ecologico de Castilla y Leon

wines.description: The estate Reserva from Jorge Monzon's certified-organic winery at La Aguilera, Burgos. Old Tinto Fino vines in large French oak foudres produce a mineral, structured wine of real terroir character.

wines.name: Dominio del Aguila Gran Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Extended ageing in large French oak foudres of 500 litres and above

pairings.why: The wine's intensity and age demand the grandest Castilian roasts.
Long-aged cheese mirrors the wine's mineral depth and dried-fruit complexity.
Earthy truffle and mushroom resonate with the wine's forest floor register.

history.summary: The Gran Reserva from Dominio del Aguila is produced only in exceptional years from the estate's oldest organically farmed parcels and undergoes extended ageing in large foudres. First produced with the 2012 vintage.

milestones.event: First Dominio del Aguila Gran Reserva produced from oldest organic plots

wines.description: Dominio del Aguila's top-tier Gran Reserva, produced only in exceptional vintages from the oldest certified-organic Tinto Fino parcels at La Aguilera, Burgos. Extended foudre ageing produces a wine of profound mineral complexity.

wines.name: Dominio del Aguila Albillo Mayor
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged in old French oak barrels

pairings.why: The wine's mineral acidity and stone fruit complement delicate freshwater fish.
Saline mineral notes in the wine echo the cured ham and asparagus pairing.
Gentle creaminess of young Spanish sheep's milk cheese works with the wine's texture.

history.summary: Albillo Mayor is the indigenous white grape of Ribera del Duero historically grown in mixed plantings alongside Tinto Fino. Jorge Monzon began producing a separate varietal bottling from his certified-organic old-vine parcels at La Aguilera, one of very few white wines made under the DO.

milestones.event: First Dominio del Aguila Albillo Mayor white produced from old-vine organic parcels

wines.description: One of very few white wines produced under DO Ribera del Duero, this Albillo Mayor from Jorge Monzon's certified-organic estate at La Aguilera shows stone fruit, almond and saline mineral character from high-altitude old vines.

wines.name: Emilio Moro Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French and American oak barrels

pairings.why: A classic Ribera del Duero pairing; the wine's fruit and oak spice complement the meat.
The wine's medium body and cherry fruit work with caramelised pork ribs.
Sheep's milk cheese amplifies the wine's fruit and vanilla notes.

history.summary: Bodegas Emilio Moro was commercially established in 1987, when the Moro family began labelling and selling their wine commercially after generations of grape growing in Pesquera de Duero. The Crianza is the foundation wine of the estate.

milestones.event: Bodegas Emilio Moro formally established as a commercial winery

wines.description: The foundation Crianza of the four-generation Emilio Moro family estate at Pesquera de Duero, founded commercially in 1987. 100% Tinto Fino with 12 months in mixed oak and an approachable, fruit-forward style.

verified.address_quoted: Ctra. Peñafiel-Valoria, S/N, 47315 Pesquera de Duero, Valladolid

wines.name: Malleolus
wines.oak_regime: 16 months in French oak barriques

pairings.why: The wine's ripe fruit and firm structure are a textbook match for slow-roasted Castilian lamb.
Dark fruit and cedar in the wine amplify charred beef and fresh herbs.
Semi-aged hard cheese mirrors the wine's structure and fruit depth.

history.summary: Malleolus was launched in 1999 as the Emilio Moro estate's prestige level wine, taking its name from the Latin diminutive of Tempranillo ('small mallet', referring to the grape cluster shape). It draws on the oldest and best-positioned Tinto Fino parcels across the estate.

milestones.event: Malleolus first vintage produced from selected old Tinto Fino vines

wines.description: Emilio Moro's prestige estate blend from selected old Tinto Fino vines, first produced in 1999. Named after the Latin root for Tempranillo, it is aged 16 months in French oak for ripe dark fruit, mineral depth and firm structure.

verified.address_quoted: Ctra. Peñafiel-Valoria, S/N, 47315 Pesquera de Duero, Valladolid

wines.name: Malleolus de Valderramiro
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: The wine's power and mineral complexity are scaled to the grandest Castilian roasts.
Game and dark berry in the wine create a rich harmony.
Long-aged hard cheese amplifies the wine's mineral and dark-fruit intensity.

history.summary: Malleolus de Valderramiro is Emilio Moro's single-vineyard flagship, sourced from the Valderramiro plot at Pesquera de Duero. First produced with the 2000 vintage, it represents the pinnacle of the estate in a single Tinto Fino parcel.

milestones.event: Malleolus de Valderramiro first produced from the Valderramiro single vineyard

wines.description: Emilio Moro's single-vineyard prestige cuvee from the Valderramiro parcel at Pesquera de Duero, first produced in 2000. Aged 18 months in new French oak, it delivers extraordinary mineral depth and ageing potential.

verified.address_quoted: Ctra. Peñafiel-Valoria, S/N, 47315 Pesquera de Duero, Valladolid

wines.name: Malleolus de Sanchomartín
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: Only the grandest Castilian roasts are a worthy match for this wine's scale.
The wine's dark mineral core complements earthy, gamey braises.
Truffle earthiness and aged sheep's milk cheese echo the wine's mineral and dark-fruit register.

history.summary: Malleolus de Sanchomartín was first produced with the 2001 vintage from the Sanchomartín single-vineyard parcel, which the Moro family regards as their most mineral and structured site. It is the companion to Valderramiro in the estate's single-vineyard tier.

milestones.event: Malleolus de Sanchomartín first vintage from the Sanchomartín single-vineyard plot

wines.description: Emilio Moro's most powerful single-vineyard cuvee, from the Sanchomartín plot, first produced in 2001. Aged 18 months in new French oak, it delivers extreme mineral concentration and very long ageing potential.

verified.address_quoted: Ctra. Peñafiel-Valoria, S/N, 47315 Pesquera de Duero, Valladolid

wines.name: El Zarzal
wines.oak_regime: Aged in used French oak for a lighter touch

pairings.why: Light and fresh fruit pairs easily with simply grilled Castilian lamb.
Spiced sausage and rustic bread with the wine's fresh red fruit is a classic Spanish combination.
Fresh sheep's milk cheese and the wine's cherry notes are a casual, easy match.

history.summary: El Zarzal was introduced by Emilio Moro as an everyday drinking wine from younger-vine Tinto Fino, designed for immediate enjoyment and accessible pricing while keeping the estate's Castilian character.

milestones.event: El Zarzal first released as Emilio Moro's everyday drinking label

wines.description: Emilio Moro's everyday Tinto Fino label, designed for fresh, immediate drinking with red cherry fruit, herbs and light mineral character at an accessible price point.

verified.address_quoted: Ctra. Peñafiel-Valoria, S/N, 47315 Pesquera de Duero, Valladolid

wines.name: Pago de Carraovejas Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French and American oak barrels

pairings.dish: Roast lamb with garlic and herbs
pairings.why: A classic Ribera del Duero pairing; the wine's oak and fruit complement traditional roast lamb.
Richer, modern preparation of Castilian lamb works with the wine's fruity, accessible style.

pairings.dish: Grilled red peppers stuffed with meat
pairings.why: The wine's fruit and spice notes resonate with the pimento and meat combination.

history.summary: Pago de Carraovejas was founded in 1987 near Peñafiel and released its first Crianza with the 1988 vintage. The estate quickly became known for its Bordeaux-influenced blend of Tinto Fino with Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. The property also hosts the Michelin-starred Ambivium restaurant.

milestones.event: Pago de Carraovejas estate founded near Peñafiel
First Pago de Carraovejas Crianza released

wines.description: The entry Crianza from Pago de Carraovejas near Peñafiel, a Bordeaux-influenced blend of Tinto Fino, Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. Rich dark fruit and vanilla oak for immediate pleasure.

wines.name: Pago de Carraovejas Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 16-18 months in French oak barriques

pairings.why: The wine's structure and oak complexity match the richness of Castilian cochinillo.
Dark fruit and cedar are a natural complement to charred beef.
The hard sheep's milk cheese amplifies the wine's dark fruit and cedar finish.

history.summary: The Pago de Carraovejas Reserva has been produced since the early 1990s as a step up in oak time and complexity from the Crianza. The estate near Peñafiel has grown to become one of the most visited wineries in Ribera del Duero, partly due to its Michelin-starred Ambivium restaurant.

milestones.event: Pago de Carraovejas estate founded near Peñafiel
Reserva first produced with additional French-oak ageing

wines.description: The Reserva from Pago de Carraovejas near Peñafiel, a Bordeaux-influenced blend with extended French-oak ageing for concentrated black fruit, cedar and a firm, mineral finish.

wines.name: El Anejon de Carraovejas
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: The wine's power and complexity require the most celebratory of Castilian roasts.
Earthy, mineral notes in the wine resonate with game and mushroom depth.

pairings.dish: Aged Manchego Viejo or Torta del Casar
pairings.why: Intense, aged Spanish cheeses can stand up to this wine's concentrated character.

history.summary: El Anejon de Carraovejas was launched in 1999 as the estate's prestige single-vineyard offering, named for the oldest parcels on the property. It represents the culmination of the estate's work in Bordeaux-influenced winemaking.

milestones.event: El Anejon de Carraovejas first vintage, the estate's prestige single-vineyard wine

wines.description: Pago de Carraovejas's top-tier single-vineyard prestige cuvee from the estate's oldest parcels near Peñafiel, first produced in 1999. Aged 22 months in new French oak for extraordinary concentration and longevity.

wines.name: Cuesta de las Liebres
wines.oak_regime: 16 months in French oak

pairings.why: The wine's minerality and dark fruit work with herb-grilled lamb.
The wine's structure supports the richness of roasted pork with fermented garlic.
The contrast of salt and sweet against the wine's mineral fruit is a classic Spanish pairing.

history.summary: Cuesta de las Liebres is a single-vineyard Tinto Fino from the slope named for the hares (liebres) that live on the estate hillside. It was introduced in 2008 to showcase the minerality of a specific cooler parcel.

milestones.event: Cuesta de las Liebres first vintage, showcasing the named single-vineyard slope

wines.description: A 100% Tinto Fino single-vineyard wine from the Cuesta de las Liebres slope at Pago de Carraovejas near Peñafiel, first produced in 2008. Minerally and structured, with dark fruit and fresh acidity.

wines.name: Arzuaga Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in American and French oak barrels

pairings.why: The wine's fruit and oak spice pair naturally with Castilian roast lamb.
Dark fruit and medium tannins complement charred beef dishes.
Spiced sausage and hard sheep's cheese echo the wine's spice and fruit notes.

history.summary: Bodegas Arzuaga Navarro was founded in 1993 at Quintanilla de Onesimo, combining wine production with hospitality from the outset. The Crianza is the estate's most widely distributed wine.

milestones.event: Bodegas Arzuaga Navarro founded at Quintanilla de Onesimo; first Crianza produced

wines.description: The estate Crianza from the Arzuaga family winery at Quintanilla de Onesimo, founded in 1993. A blend of Tinto Fino, Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot with 14 months in mixed oak for approachable dark fruit and vanilla.

wines.name: Arzuaga Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak barriques

pairings.why: The wine's structure and dark fruit stand up to the rich fat and delicate meat of cochinillo.
Braised lamb with the wine's tobacco and spice notes is a classic Castilian combination.
Firm cheese and the wine's mineral backbone are mutually enhancing.

history.summary: The Arzuaga Reserva has been produced from the mid-1990s as the estate's premium tier, offering greater oak ageing and complexity than the Crianza.

milestones.event: Bodegas Arzuaga Navarro founded
First Arzuaga Reserva produced

wines.description: The Reserva from Bodegas Arzuaga Navarro at Quintanilla de Onesimo, a Tinto Fino-led blend aged 18 months in French oak. Greater structure and complexity than the Crianza.

wines.name: Gran Arzuaga
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: Only the grandest Castilian roasts suit the scale of this wine.
Game and dark berry resonate with the wine's intense fruit and mineral depth.
This pungent, aged Spanish sheep's cheese can match the wine's concentration.

history.summary: Gran Arzuaga was introduced in 2001 as the estate's prestige selection, produced only in excellent vintages from the oldest Tinto Fino and Cabernet Sauvignon vines. It represents the summit of the Arzuaga range.

milestones.event: Gran Arzuaga first vintage, the estate's prestige selection

wines.description: Gran Arzuaga is the prestige single-selection cuvee from Bodegas Arzuaga Navarro, first produced in 2001. Aged 22 months in new French oak with extraordinary concentration from the oldest Tinto Fino and Cabernet vines.

wines.name: Hacienda Monasterio Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak barriques

pairings.why: The wine's Bordeaux-influenced structure and dark fruit are ideal with slow-cooked Castilian lamb.
Dark fruit and cedar complement charred beef with herb condiments.
Quince paste and hard sheep's cheese highlight the wine's dark fruit and structure.

history.summary: Hacienda Monasterio was founded in 1991 near Pesquera de Duero with Peter Sisseck, later of Pingus fame, as the consulting winemaker. The estate's distinctive five-variety Bordeaux-inspired blend set it apart from the mono-varietal tradition of Ribera del Duero.

milestones.event: Hacienda Monasterio founded near Pesquera de Duero; Peter Sisseck joins as winemaker
First Hacienda Monasterio Reserva produced

wines.description: The flagship Reserva from Hacienda Monasterio near Pesquera de Duero, founded in 1991 with Peter Sisseck as winemaker. A five-variety Bordeaux-style blend aged 18 months in French oak.

wines.name: Hacienda Monasterio Reserva Especial
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: This wine's intensity requires the grandest Castilian occasion dishes.

pairings.dish: Wild mushroom and black truffle risotto
pairings.why: Truffle and mushroom earthiness resonate with the wine's mineral depth.
Pungent, concentrated Spanish cheese is a worthy partner for this wine's intensity.

history.summary: Hacienda Monasterio Reserva Especial was introduced in 2001 as a prestige single-selection from the estate's oldest and finest parcels, produced only in exceptional vintages with extended new-oak ageing.

milestones.event: Hacienda Monasterio founded
Reserva Especial first produced as the estate's prestige tier

wines.description: Hacienda Monasterio's prestige Reserva Especial, first produced in 2001 from the estate's finest parcels. Extended new French-oak ageing delivers intense concentration, Bordeaux-influenced structure and outstanding longevity.

wines.name: Vina Sastre Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French oak barrels

pairings.why: Burgos-style grilled lamb and the wine's bright cherry fruit and herbaceous notes are a natural match.
The wine's medium body and herb character pair with simple herb-roasted poultry.
Fresh sheep's milk cheese brings out the wine's cherry and herb profile.

history.summary: Bodegas Hermanos Sastre was established in 1992 at La Horra by the Sastre brothers, who had been farming Tinto Fino vines for generations. The estate's Crianza has become a benchmark for the bright, fresh style of the Burgos sector.

milestones.event: Bodegas Hermanos Sastre formally established at La Horra, Burgos

wines.description: The estate Crianza from Bodegas Hermanos Sastre at La Horra, Burgos. 100% Tinto Fino aged 12 months in French oak, with bright cherry fruit, dried herbs and the characteristic freshness of the Burgos high-altitude sub-zone.

wines.name: Pesus
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: Only the grandest regional dishes are a worthy match for this wine's scale and intensity.

pairings.dish: Wild game with dark berry sauce
pairings.why: Earthy game and the wine's mineral depth create a profound pairing.
Concentrated cheese and truffle earthiness match the wine's mineral concentration.

history.summary: Pesus was first produced in 1997 from the Sastre brothers' finest old-vine Tinto Fino plots at La Horra. In tiny production and released years after the harvest, it quickly attracted a cult following among Burgos-sector collectors.

milestones.event: First vintage of Pesus produced from old Tinto Fino vines at La Horra

wines.description: Pesus is the cult single-vineyard Tinto Fino from Bodegas Hermanos Sastre at La Horra, first produced in 1997. In tiny production with 22 months in new French oak, it is one of the Burgos sector's most sought-after wines.

wines.name: Goyo Tinto del Pais
wines.oak_regime: Aged in old French oak barrels with minimal intervention

taste.summary: A natural-wine Tinto Fino from pre-phylloxera ungrafted old vines at Villalbilla de Gumiel, with wild cherry, earthy mineral and high acidity from minimal-intervention winemaking.

pairings.why: The wine's bright acidity and earthy notes complement herb-roasted pork without overwhelming it.
The freshness and tannin-light profile are ideal for simple, charcoal-grilled lamb.
The wine's acidity and cherry fruit work with the sweetness of quince and hard cheese.

history.summary: Goyo Garcia Viadero began producing wine commercially in 2001 at Villalbilla de Gumiel, using pre-phylloxera ungrafted Tinto Fino and Albillo Mayor vines. The estate became a reference for natural wine production in Ribera del Duero, working with minimal sulfur and no fining or filtration.

milestones.event: Goyo Garcia Viadero first vintage from pre-phylloxera old vines at Villalbilla de Gumiel

wines.description: Goyo Garcia Viadero's flagship natural Tinto Fino from pre-phylloxera ungrafted old vines at Villalbilla de Gumiel in the Burgos sector, first produced in 2001. Minimal intervention, no filtration, wild cherry and earthy mineral character.

wines.name: Vallunco
wines.oak_regime: Aged in old French barrels and amphora for natural fermentation

taste.summary: A single-parcel natural-wine Tinto Fino from the Vallunco plot, with greater depth and precision than the estate Goyo, showing wild berry, earthy mineral and vivid acidity from old ungrafted vines.

pairings.why: The wine's freshness and acidity cut through the richness of stuffed peppers.
The delicate body and bright acidity suit simpler roasted poultry.
Delicate dairy and the wine's earthy acidity create a light, elegant combination.

history.summary: Vallunco is Goyo Garcia Viadero's single-parcel natural wine from the named Vallunco plot, first produced with the 2005 harvest. It is sourced from some of the estate's oldest ungrafted Tinto Fino vines.

milestones.event: Vallunco single-parcel natural wine first produced from the Vallunco plot

wines.description: Goyo Garcia Viadero's single-parcel natural wine from the Vallunco plot, first produced in 2005 from ungrafted old Tinto Fino vines. Wild berry, earthy mineral and racy acidity from a natural-wine pioneer in the Burgos sector.

wines.name: Dominio de Atauta
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French oak barrels, proportion new

pairings.why: The wine's mineral and dark-fruit intensity complement charcoal-grilled lamb at altitude.
Classic Soria lamb and old-vine Tinto Fino is a deeply traditional regional pairing.
Earthy umami from wild mushrooms resonates with the wine's mineral and herbal register.

history.summary: Dominio de Atauta was established in 2000 to work with the extraordinary old, pre-phylloxera ungrafted Tinto Fino vineyards in the village of Atauta in the Soria sub-zone, the highest and most extreme terroir in the appellation.

milestones.event: Dominio de Atauta founded to work with pre-phylloxera ungrafted Tinto Fino in Soria sub-zone

wines.description: The estate Tinto Fino from Dominio de Atauta, founded in 2000 in the extreme-altitude Soria sub-zone. Pre-phylloxera ungrafted vines at over 1000 metres produce dark mineral character and high acidity.

wines.name: Llanos del Almendro
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak, proportion new

pairings.dish: Roast leg of lamb with garlic and rosemary
pairings.why: The wine's concentrated mineral character and high tannins suit herb-seasoned roast lamb.
Gamey iron richness echoes the wine's dark, earthy mineral profile.
Long-aged hard cheese amplifies the wine's dark fruit and mineral finish.

history.summary: Llanos del Almendro is one of Dominio de Atauta's single-parcel cuvees from the Atauta plateau, first produced with the 2004 vintage from old ungrafted vines on the Llanos del Almendro site.

milestones.event: Llanos del Almendro single-parcel cuvee first produced from old ungrafted vines

wines.description: A single-parcel Tinto Fino from the Llanos del Almendro site at Atauta in the Soria sub-zone, first produced in 2004 from old ungrafted vines. Greater concentration and mineral depth than the estate wine.

wines.name: La Mala
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: This wine's power calls for the richest expression of the region's most iconic ingredient.

pairings.dish: Wild boar braised with black truffle
pairings.why: Game and truffle earthiness mirror the wine's extreme mineral depth.

pairings.dish: Aged Torta del Casar or Manchego Viejo
pairings.why: Only the most concentrated Spanish cheeses can match this wine's intensity.

history.summary: La Mala is Dominio de Atauta's single-parcel flagship, produced from the estate's most exceptional old-vine plot at Atauta. First produced in 2005, it is the most concentrated expression of the Soria sub-zone's extreme-altitude Tinto Fino.

milestones.event: La Mala first vintage produced from the most exceptional single plot at Atauta

wines.description: La Mala is the prestige single-parcel flagship of Dominio de Atauta, from the estate's finest old-vine Tinto Fino site at Atauta in the Soria sub-zone. First produced in 2005, it achieves extraordinary mineral concentration and longevity.

wines.name: Capellanes Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French and American oak

pairings.why: Classic Ribera pairing with the wine's dark fruit and oak spice.
Dark fruit and medium tannins complement charred beef.
Hard sheep's milk cheese echoes the wine's spice and vanilla.

history.summary: Pago de los Capellanes was founded in 1996 at Pedrosa de Duero, taking its name from an old communal land parcel historically owned by church chaplains (capellanes) in the village. The Crianza is the estate's most accessible wine.

milestones.event: Pago de los Capellanes founded at Pedrosa de Duero; first Crianza produced

wines.description: The estate Crianza from Pago de los Capellanes at Pedrosa de Duero, founded in 1996. A Tinto Fino and Cabernet Sauvignon blend with 12 months in mixed oak for approachable dark fruit and vanilla.

wines.name: Capellanes El Picon
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: The wine's power demands the grandest Castilian occasion meats.
Gamey richness and dark berry sauce echo the wine's intense fruit and mineral core.
Concentrated hard cheese is a worthy textural and flavour match.

history.summary: El Picon is the Pago de los Capellanes prestige wine, first produced with the 2001 vintage from the estate's finest old Tinto Fino plot of the same name, aged in new French oak and released only in exceptional years.

milestones.event: El Picon first vintage produced from the named single-vineyard plot at Pedrosa de Duero

wines.description: El Picon is the prestige single-vineyard Tinto Fino from Pago de los Capellanes, first produced in 2001 from the El Picon plot at Pedrosa de Duero. Aged 22 months in new French oak for extraordinary mineral concentration.

wines.name: Capellanes El Nogal
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak barriques

pairings.why: Slow-cooked lamb and the wine's dark fruit and mineral notes complement each other.
Earthy legume stew and the wine's mineral and herb register are a rustic match.
Hard sheep's milk cheese and the wine's structure create a balanced pairing.

history.summary: El Nogal (named for the walnut tree on the vineyard) is a second single-vineyard cuvee from Pago de los Capellanes, positioned in quality and oak regime between the estate Reserva and the prestige El Picon. First produced in 2003.

milestones.event: El Nogal first vintage produced from the named single-vineyard parcel

wines.description: A single-vineyard Tinto Fino from the El Nogal parcel at Pago de los Capellanes, Pedrosa de Duero. First produced in 2003, positioned between the estate Reserva and the top-tier El Picon in concentration and oak treatment.

wines.name: Matallana
wines.oak_regime: 16-18 months in French oak barriques, mostly new

taste.summary: Telmo Rodriguez's single-vineyard flagship from old Tinto Fino parcels in the Valladolid sector, applying his philosophy of terroir transparency and minimal intervention to produce a concentrated, mineral, age-worthy red.

pairings.dish: Roast leg of lamb with thyme
pairings.why: The wine's power and precision call for traditional Castilian herb-seasoned roasts.
Game and the wine's intense mineral-dark fruit character are a natural combination.
Dense aged cheese mirrors the wine's concentrated mineral and dark fruit.

history.summary: Telmo Rodriguez, the itinerant Spanish winemaker behind Compania de Vinos Telmo Rodriguez, launched his Ribera del Duero project with the 1999 vintage, selecting old-vine Tinto Fino parcels in the Valladolid sector near Quintanilla de Onesimo for a single-vineyard wine produced with minimal intervention.

milestones.event: Matallana first vintage produced by Telmo Rodriguez from old Tinto Fino parcels in Valladolid sector

wines.description: Telmo Rodriguez's flagship single-vineyard Ribera del Duero wine from old Tinto Fino parcels near Quintanilla de Onesimo, first produced in 1999. Concentrated, mineral and age-worthy with long finish.

wines.name: Gazur
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in used French oak barrels

pairings.why: Fresh acidity and medium body pair naturally with simply grilled lamb.
The lighter style is versatile enough for simple roasted poultry.
A casual Castilian pairing with the wine's cherry fruit and mineral notes.

history.summary: Gazur is the more accessible second wine of the Telmo Rodriguez Ribera del Duero project, first produced in 2002 to provide a complementary entry-level expression alongside the prestige Matallana.

milestones.event: Gazur first produced as the accessible second wine of the Matallana project

wines.description: The accessible second wine of Telmo Rodriguez's Ribera del Duero project, first produced in 2002. Medium-bodied Tinto Fino with fresh cherry, herbs and mineral character at an approachable price.

wines.name: Protos Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in American oak barrels

pairings.why: The classic pairing for this Castilian region; the wine's fruit and oak complement the lamb.
The rich, crispy pork and the wine's vanilla and cherry notes are a Segovia tradition.
Casual, rustic Castilian eating pairs naturally with this approachable Crianza.

history.summary: Bodegas Protos was founded in 1927 as a cooperative under the walls of Peñafiel castle, making it the oldest cooperative winery in Ribera del Duero and one of the founding estates of the DO in 1982. The Crianza is the estate's most widely available wine.

milestones.event: Bodegas Protos founded as a cooperative under Peñafiel castle
DO Ribera del Duero granted; Protos among the founding estates
New Richard Rogers-designed cellar opens at Peñafiel

wines.description: The Crianza from historic Bodegas Protos, founded in 1927 at Peñafiel. The appellation's oldest cooperative producer, housed since 2008 in a Richard Rogers-designed cellar, delivers classic Tinto Fino with American oak vanilla and ripe cherry fruit.

wines.name: Protos Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 18-20 months in American and French oak barrels

pairings.why: Rich fat-crisped cochinillo and the wine's tobacco and dark fruit are a traditional Castilian pairing.
Extended braising and the wine's leather and dried-fruit complexity are mutually complementary.
Hard sheep's milk cheese amplifies the wine's traditional Castilian character.

history.summary: Protos Reserva has been produced since the establishment of DO Ribera del Duero in 1982, adding extended oak ageing to the Crianza style. It represents the more classic, structured expression of the estate's Tinto Fino.

milestones.event: Bodegas Protos founded
DO Ribera del Duero granted; Protos Reserva first produced

wines.description: Protos Reserva from the historic 1927-founded cooperative at Peñafiel, aged 18-20 months in mixed oak. Classic Tinto Fino character with dark fruit, tobacco, leather and firm structure.

wines.name: Protos Seleccion
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: The wine's power and complexity match the grandest Castilian occasion dishes.
Earthy, mineral notes in the wine echo the truffle and mushroom richness.
Concentrated, pungent sheep's cheese is a worthy partner for this wine's intensity.

history.summary: Protos Seleccion was introduced in 2005 as a modern premium tier from the historic Peñafiel cooperative, using selected old Tinto Fino parcels and new French oak to create a more ambitious wine than the traditional American-oak range.

milestones.event: Bodegas Protos founded
Protos Seleccion first produced as a modern premium tier with new French oak

wines.description: Protos Seleccion is the modern prestige cuvee of historic Bodegas Protos, introduced in 2005. Selected old Tinto Fino parcels aged 22 months in new French oak deliver concentrated dark fruit, mineral depth and long ageing potential.

wines.name: Protos Finca El Grajo Viejo
wines.oak_regime: 20 months in new and second-fill French oak

pairings.dish: Roast lamb with garlic and herbs
pairings.why: The wine's mineral concentration suits traditional Castilian herb-roasted lamb.
Earthy, gamey braises resonate with the wine's mineral and dark-fruit depth.

pairings.dish: Aged Manchego with olive oil and olives
pairings.why: The wine's mineral finish finds a natural partner in the salt of aged cheese and olives.

history.summary: Protos Finca El Grajo Viejo was introduced in 2009 as the estate's single-vineyard flagship, drawing on the oldest Tinto Fino vines from the El Grajo Viejo parcel. It represents a departure from the cooperative's classic American-oak style toward a more terroir-focused expression.

milestones.event: Bodegas Protos founded at Peñafiel
Finca El Grajo Viejo single-vineyard cuvee first produced

wines.description: The single-vineyard flagship of Bodegas Protos from the El Grajo Viejo old-vine plot, first produced in 2009. A more terroir-focused departure from the cooperative's classic style, with concentrated dark fruit, mineral depth and high ageing potential.

wines.name: Felix Callejo Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in French and American oak barrels

pairings.why: The bright acidity and medium body pair naturally with charcoal-grilled Burgos lamb.
Spiced sausage in cider matches the wine's herb and spice register.
Fresh sheep's milk cheese and the wine's cherry notes are an easy, pleasant pairing.

history.summary: Jose Felix Callejo planted the estate's first vines at Sotillo de la Ribera in the late 1980s and began commercial production around 1990. The estate achieved certified organic status and has passed management to daughter Noelia Callejo, who has focused on high-altitude expression.

milestones.event: First Felix Callejo wines produced at Sotillo de la Ribera, Burgos
Organic certification achieved by Bodegas Felix Callejo

wines.description: An organic Tinto Fino Crianza from Bodegas Felix Callejo at Sotillo de la Ribera in the Burgos high-altitude sub-zone. Fresh cherry fruit, herbs and bright acidity from 830-930 metre limestone soils.

verified.address_quoted: Avenida del Cid, Km. 16, 09441 Sotillo de la Ribera, Burgos, Spain

wines.name: Felix Callejo Gran Callejo
wines.oak_regime: 20 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: The wine's mineral intensity and firm tannins are ideally suited to rich, slow-cooked lamb.
Gamey iron richness and the wine's dark berry mineral character are complementary.
Hard sheep's milk cheese mirrors the wine's concentration and mineral backbone.

history.summary: Gran Callejo is Bodegas Felix Callejo's prestige selection from old-vine certified-organic Tinto Fino at the highest altitudes of the Sotillo de la Ribera terroir, first produced with the 2002 vintage.

milestones.event: Gran Callejo first vintage produced from the oldest, highest-altitude organic Tinto Fino parcels

wines.description: The prestige organic Tinto Fino from Bodegas Felix Callejo, first produced in 2002. From the oldest high-altitude vines at Sotillo de la Ribera, Burgos, aged 20 months in new French oak with extraordinary mineral concentration.

verified.address_quoted: Avenida del Cid, Km. 16, 09441 Sotillo de la Ribera, Burgos, Spain

wines.name: Carmelo Rodero Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in American and French oak barrels

pairings.why: Approachable fruit and spice pair well with simply cooked Castilian lamb.
The wine's medium body and fruit complement herb-seasoned pork.
Hard sheep's milk cheese brings out the wine's fruit and vanilla character.

history.summary: Bodegas Carmelo Rodero was founded in 1990 at Pedrosa de Duero by Carmelo Rodero, and the family has continued to manage the estate through three generations, producing a range from Crianza through to the prestige TSM.

milestones.event: Bodegas Carmelo Rodero founded at Pedrosa de Duero, Burgos

wines.description: The estate Crianza from the Rodero family at Pedrosa de Duero, Burgos. A Tinto Fino and Cabernet Sauvignon blend aged 12 months in mixed oak with approachable dark cherry, vanilla and Castilian character.

wines.name: Carmelo Rodero TSM
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: The wine's power and concentration call for the most celebratory Castilian roasts.
Earthy, rich game complements the wine's mineral dark-fruit intensity.

pairings.dish: Aged Torta del Casar or Manchego Viejo
pairings.why: Concentrated aged Spanish cheese stands up to the wine's intensity and structure.

history.summary: Carmelo Rodero TSM (for Tinto Fino, Syrah/Merlot blend) was introduced in 2002 as the estate's prestige Bordeaux-influenced cuvee, aged in new French oak for maximum concentration from the Burgos sector's finest plots.

milestones.event: Carmelo Rodero TSM first vintage, the estate's prestige Bordeaux-style blend

wines.description: Carmelo Rodero TSM is the prestige Bordeaux-influenced blend from Bodegas Carmelo Rodero, first produced in 2002 at Pedrosa de Duero. Aged 22 months in new French oak with concentrated dark fruit and high ageing potential.

wines.name: Vina Pedrosa Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12-14 months in American and French oak barrels

pairings.why: Classic Ribera del Duero pairing; the wine's fruit and spice complement roast lamb.
Medium tannins and dark fruit work with grilled beef dishes.
Easy, rustic Spanish pairing with the wine's cherry and vanilla notes.

history.summary: Bodegas Perez Pascuas was founded in 1980 at Pedrosa de Duero by three brothers from the Perez Pascuas family, who had grown grapes in the village for generations. The Vina Pedrosa Crianza was among the first wines produced commercially and carries the name of the home village.

milestones.event: Bodegas Perez Pascuas founded at Pedrosa de Duero by the three Perez Pascuas brothers

wines.description: The estate Crianza from Bodegas Perez Pascuas at Pedrosa de Duero, founded in 1980. A Tinto Fino and Cabernet Sauvignon blend aged in mixed oak with approachable dark cherry, vanilla and Castilian character.

wines.name: Vina Pedrosa Gran Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 24 months in French and American oak followed by extended bottle ageing

pairings.why: Extended ageing and the wine's tobacco and leather tones suit the richness of slow-cooked Castilian lamb.
Earthy game and the wine's mature complexity are complementary.
Firm Spanish hard cheese and the wine's traditional Castilian structure create a harmonious pairing.

history.summary: Vina Pedrosa Gran Reserva has been produced since 1983, representing the Perez Pascuas estate's commitment to long ageing in the classic Castilian tradition. It is released years after the harvest and reflects the full maturation potential of the Burgos sector.

milestones.event: Bodegas Perez Pascuas founded
First Vina Pedrosa Gran Reserva produced with extended oak and bottle ageing

wines.description: The Gran Reserva from Bodegas Perez Pascuas at Pedrosa de Duero, first produced in 1983. Aged 24 months in mixed oak with extended bottle ageing, showing dried cherry, tobacco and leather in the classic Castilian long-ageing style.

wines.name: Perez Pascuas Gran Reserva Gran Seleccion
wines.oak_regime: 28 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: The wine's power and complexity demand the grandest traditional Castilian occasion dishes.

pairings.dish: Wild boar or venison with dark sauce
pairings.why: Gamey richness and intense mineral-dark-fruit character are a natural match.
Concentrated aged Spanish cheeses can stand up to this wine's intensity.

history.summary: Perez Pascuas Gran Reserva Gran Seleccion was first produced in 1999 as the estate's ultra-premium single-selection offering, made only in the finest vintages from the oldest Tinto Fino vines.

milestones.event: Bodegas Perez Pascuas founded at Pedrosa de Duero
Gran Reserva Gran Seleccion first produced as ultra-premium single-selection

wines.description: The ultra-premium single selection from Bodegas Perez Pascuas at Pedrosa de Duero, first produced in 1999. Old Tinto Fino vines aged 28 months in new French oak in exceptional vintages only, with extraordinary concentration and longevity.

wines.name: Valdubon Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in American and French oak barrels

taste.summary: An accessible Tinto Fino Crianza from a Milagros estate in the Burgos sector, with approachable cherry fruit, vanilla and smooth tannins from the cave-cellar winery.

pairings.why: Approachable fruit and vanilla oak pair naturally with simple Castilian lamb.
The wine's medium body and fruit complement herb-seasoned grilled pork.
Fresh sheep's milk cheese and the wine's cherry notes are a casual match.

history.summary: Bodegas Valdubon was founded in 1999 at Milagros in the Burgos sector, featuring a distinctive cave-cellar dug into the hillside under the winery. The Crianza is the estate's most accessible wine.

milestones.event: Bodegas Valdubon founded at Milagros, Burgos; first Crianza produced

wines.description: The estate Crianza from Bodegas Valdubon at Milagros in the Burgos sector, founded in 1999. 100% Tinto Fino aged in mixed oak with approachable cherry fruit, vanilla and smooth tannins.

wines.name: Valdubon Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 16 months in French oak barriques

taste.summary: The Valdubon Reserva with extended French-oak ageing, showing concentrated dark fruit, cedar and a more structured profile from the high-altitude Burgos sector.

pairings.why: The wine's structure and dark fruit stand up to the richness of Castilian cochinillo.
Herb and garlic lamb and the wine's spice and herb notes are a natural match.
Hard sheep's milk cheese and the wine's mineral backbone create balance.

history.summary: Valdubon Reserva was first produced in 2001 with extended French-oak ageing as a step up in quality from the Crianza at the Milagros estate.

milestones.event: Bodegas Valdubon founded
Valdubon Reserva first produced

wines.description: Valdubon Reserva from the Burgos-sector estate at Milagros, first produced in 2001. 100% Tinto Fino aged 16 months in French oak for concentrated dark fruit, cedar and firm structure.

wines.name: Cepa 21
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French oak barrels

pairings.why: Fresh acidity and dark fruit pair well with charcoal-grilled Castilian lamb.
Paprika-seasoned pork and the wine's herb and fruit notes are a rustic match.
Fresh sheep's milk cheese and the wine's fresh cherry notes are a casual pairing.

history.summary: Bodegas Cepa 21 was founded in 2001 as the Moro family's second estate at Pesquera de Duero, focusing on younger-vine Tinto Fino to create more vibrant, accessible wines than the prestige Malleolus range of Emilio Moro. The Cepa 21 label was first produced with the 2003 vintage.

milestones.event: Bodegas Cepa 21 founded by the Moro family as their second estate
First Cepa 21 vintage produced

wines.description: The flagship Tinto Fino from the Moro family's second estate at Pesquera de Duero, Bodegas Cepa 21. First produced in 2003 with younger-vine fruit for a vibrant, fresh style.

wines.name: Malabrigo
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: Concentrated dark fruit and firm tannins match slow-roasted Castilian lamb.
The wine's structure and dark fruit are ideal with charred beef and garlic.
Firm sheep's milk cheese and the wine's mineral and dark fruit notes create balance.

history.summary: Malabrigo is Bodegas Cepa 21's premium selection, introduced in 2005 from the estate's oldest and most selected Tinto Fino plots at Pesquera de Duero. It represents a step up in concentration and oak from the estate Cepa 21.

milestones.event: Malabrigo first produced from selected old Tinto Fino parcels at Pesquera de Duero

wines.description: Malabrigo is the premium selection of Bodegas Cepa 21, first produced in 2005 from selected old Tinto Fino parcels at Pesquera de Duero. Aged 18 months in new French oak for concentrated dark fruit and firm mineral structure.

wines.name: Tamaral Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in American and French oak barrels

taste.summary: An approachable, value-driven Tinto Fino Crianza from a Peñafiel area estate, with cherry fruit, vanilla and easy-drinking character that provides a reliable introduction to the appellation.

pairings.why: The light body and fresh fruit pair naturally with simple roasted poultry.
Medium tannins and fruit notes work with grilled pork at an everyday level.
Young sheep's milk cheese and the wine's cherry notes are an easy, casual pairing.
