history.summary: Neo Prieto is the early-drinking label from Bodegas Neo, aimed at everyday enjoyment. It spends just 6 months in French oak to preserve the fresh cherry and raspberry character of Tinto Fino at this altitude.

milestones.event: First Neo Prieto vintage released as an accessible entry label

wines.description: Everyday Joven-style Tinto Fino from Bodegas Neo with 6 months oak influence, showing bright cherry, raspberry and fresh acidity from the eastern Burgos plateau.

wines.name: Legaris Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: Balanced Crianza from the Torres family's Legaris estate at Curiel de Duero, with ripe red berry, cherry and cedar from 12 months in mixed oak in their gravity-fed winery.

pairings.why: The wine's red fruit and cedar complement well-seasoned roast poultry.
Classic Ribera match -- the wine's body and fruit handle lamb with ease.
Everyday red fruit register pairs well with the eggy potato omelette.

history.summary: Legaris was founded in 2000 by the Torres family of Penedes in the Valladolid sector at Curiel de Duero. Their modern gravity-fed winery was designed to minimise pump-overs and handle fruit with care, with the Crianza showcasing the approach.

milestones.event: Legaris estate founded by the Torres family in Curiel de Duero
First Crianza vintage released

wines.description: Balanced Tinto Fino Crianza from the Torres family's Legaris estate at Curiel de Duero, showing red berry, cherry and cedar in a clean, polished everyday style.

wines.name: Legaris Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 16 months in French oak barriques

taste.summary: Fuller-bodied Reserva from Legaris with dark cherry, chocolate and cedar from extended time in French oak; herbal and mineral notes reflect the Curiel de Duero terroir.

pairings.why: The wine's dark fruit and firm tannins suit the fat and depth of slow lamb.
Earthy chocolate and cedar notes echo the earthy stew elements.
Hard sheep's-milk cheese salt pairs with the firm, food-friendly tannins.

history.summary: The Legaris Reserva applies the Torres family's precision gravity-flow winemaking to a longer oak regime, spending 16 months in French barriques before bottle rest in the estate's cave cellar at Curiel de Duero.

milestones.event: First Legaris Reserva released from the Curiel de Duero estate

wines.description: Fuller-bodied Reserva from the Torres family's Legaris at Curiel de Duero, aged 16 months in French oak with dark cherry, chocolate and herbal notes from the Valladolid sector limestone terroir.

wines.name: Legaris Premium
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new French oak barriques

taste.summary: Legaris Premium is a concentrated, structured single-vineyard selection aged 22 months in new French oak, with intense cassis, graphite and violet from old Tinto Fino at Curiel de Duero.

pairings.dish: Roast Iberico presa with black garlic
pairings.why: Dense tannins and dark fruit need the fat of premium Iberico cuts.
Concentration and graphite notes in the wine meet the gamey depth of wild boar.
Concentrated fruit and tannins balance the saline cheese with sweet quince.

history.summary: Legaris Premium represents the estate's most meticulous selection, drawn from the oldest Tinto Fino vines at Curiel de Duero and receiving 22 months in new French oak in the Torres family's gravity-fed cave cellar.

milestones.event: First Legaris Premium vintage released from selected old-vine parcels

wines.description: Legaris's prestige old-vine selection aged 22 months in new French oak, with concentrated cassis, graphite and fine-grain tannins from the Curiel de Duero estate.

wines.name: Trus Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: Balanced, accessible Crianza from Trus near Valladolid on the western edge of the appellation, with red cherry, plum and cedar from 12 months in mixed oak.

pairings.why: The wine's medium body and cherry fruit complement grilled lamb.
The red fruit and moderate tannins match smoky, spiced ribs.
Chorizo and potato stew pairs naturally with this everyday Ribera style.

history.summary: Bodegas Trus works from parcels on the western edge of the Ribera del Duero appellation near Valladolid, where younger soils produce Tinto Fino with bright fruit character. The Crianza has been the estate's workhorse label since 2003.

milestones.event: First Trus Crianza vintage released from western-sector parcels

wines.description: Approachable Tinto Fino Crianza from Bodegas Trus at the western edge of the Ribera del Duero appellation with red cherry, plum and cedar from 12 months in mixed oak.

wines.name: Trus Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak

taste.summary: Full-bodied Reserva from Trus with concentrated dark cherry, cedar and herbs from 18 months in French oak, with the structure to develop in bottle.

pairings.why: Dark fruit and cedar structure complement the richness of slow-cooked lamb.
Firm tannins need the protein and fat of a well-aged beef cut.
Savoury cured cheese pairs well with the wine's herbal and cedar notes.

history.summary: The Trus Reserva extends oak maturation to 18 months in French barrels, applying the western-sector Tinto Fino fruit to a structured, age-worthy style that builds complexity with bottle time.

milestones.event: First Trus Reserva released from western Ribera parcels

wines.description: Full-bodied Reserva from Bodegas Trus aged 18 months in French oak, showing dark cherry, cedar and herbal notes with structure for medium-term cellaring.

wines.name: Trus Tramonte
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new French oak barriques

taste.summary: Trus's prestige old-vine selection aged 22 months in new French oak with cassis, espresso and graphite; structured and age-worthy from the best parcels of the western sector.

pairings.dish: T-bone steak with bone marrow butter
pairings.why: Dense tannins and roasted spice need the fat of a large beef cut.
Earthy graphite notes mirror the truffle and slow-cooked gelatinous sauce.
Saline aged cheese pairs with concentrated fruit and quince echoes.

history.summary: Tramonte is Bodegas Trus's flagship label, named for a parcel on the western edge of the appellation. Only made in top years, it receives 22 months in new French oak and extended bottle rest before release.

milestones.event: First Tramonte vintage released as Trus's prestige cuvee

wines.description: Trus Tramonte is the estate's prestige old-vine selection aged 22 months in new French oak with concentrated cassis, espresso and graphite; only made in top years.

wines.name: Trus Roble
wines.oak_regime: 4 months in American oak

taste.summary: Easy-drinking Roble from Bodegas Trus with brief American oak contact, offering fresh cherry, light vanilla and bright acidity for everyday enjoyment.

pairings.why: Versatile, fresh fruit style handles a wide range of bite-sized dishes.
Light tannins and cherry fruit complement the paprika sausage.
Fresh acidity cuts through the creamy bechamel and ham filling.

history.summary: Trus Roble is the fresh, youthful entry label from Bodegas Trus, spending only 4 months in American oak to maintain bright fruit and high-toned freshness suited to everyday drinking and Spanish bar culture.

milestones.event: First Trus Roble vintage released as the estate's everyday label

wines.description: Fresh, easy-drinking Roble from Bodegas Trus with 4 months in American oak, showing bright cherry and light vanilla suited to everyday enjoyment and tapas.

wines.name: Hacienda Solano Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: Well-rounded Crianza from the high chalk plateau east of Aranda de Duero, with cherry, blackberry and vanilla from 14 months in mixed oak and approachable soft tannins.

pairings.why: The wine's medium body and cherry fruit pair beautifully with the mild, fatty Castilian roast lamb.
Blood sausage's richness is balanced by the wine's freshness and red fruit.
Approachable tannins and red fruit suit a simple pasta dish.

history.summary: Hacienda Solano sources Tinto Fino from parcels near Hontangas on the chalk-clay high plateau east of Aranda de Duero, where continental conditions produce wines with natural freshness and consistent fruit ripeness.

milestones.event: First Hacienda Solano Crianza vintage from Hontangas estate

wines.description: Well-rounded Tinto Fino Crianza from Hacienda Solano's chalk-clay plateau estate near Hontangas, with cherry, blackberry and soft vanilla from 14 months in mixed oak.

wines.name: Hacienda Solano Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 20 months in French oak

taste.summary: Structured Reserva from the Hontangas plateau with dark plum, tobacco and cedar from 20 months in French oak; the chalk-clay terroir contributes a mineral backbone.

pairings.why: The wine's firm structure and dark fruit lift this quintessential Castilian dish.
Herbaceous notes in the wine complement the seasoned lamb ribs.
Aged hard cheese amplifies the wine's mineral thread.

history.summary: The Hacienda Solano Reserva represents the estate's extended-ageing tier from Hontangas, spending 20 months in French oak before further bottle rest. The high chalk-clay plateau above Aranda de Duero supports intense, age-worthy Tinto Fino.

milestones.event: First Hacienda Solano Reserva released

wines.description: Structured Reserva from Hacienda Solano's Hontangas estate aged 20 months in French oak with dark plum, tobacco and cedar, underpinned by chalk-clay mineral character.

wines.name: Vizcarra Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: Refreshing Crianza from Mambrilla de Castrejon at 900 metres altitude, where Juan Carlos Vizcarra achieves freshness and balance rare at this elevation in the Burgos sector.

pairings.why: Fresh acidity and red fruit complement the smoky paprika roast bird.
Bay leaf and cedar in the wine echo rosemary herb seasoning.
Cherry fruit and fresh acidity complement the saline cured ham.

history.summary: Juan Carlos Vizcarra established his family estate at Mambrilla de Castrejon in the high-altitude Burgos sector around 2000, aiming for freshness and balance over power. The Crianza has been the estate's reliable benchmark label.

milestones.event: First Vizcarra Crianza vintage from Mambrilla de Castrejon

wines.description: Fresh, balanced Tinto Fino Crianza from Juan Carlos Vizcarra's high-altitude estate at Mambrilla de Castrejon in Burgos, showing cherry, plum and cedary spice with lively acidity.

wines.name: Vizcarra Senda del Oro Roble
wines.oak_regime: 5 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: Fresh, lively Roble from the high-altitude Vizcarra estate with bright cherry, strawberry and floral notes from brief oak contact and a refreshing acidity typical of the cooler Burgos elevation.

pairings.why: The fresh, fruity style is ideal for a range of light snacks.
Bright acidity complements charred courgette and red pepper.
Fresh cherry fruit and gentle tannins pair with the simple egg and potato dish.

history.summary: Senda del Oro (Golden Path) is the entry-level Roble from Bodegas Vizcarra, designed for immediate enjoyment. A short 5-month oak pass preserves the natural freshness and acidity that define Vizcarra's high-altitude site at Mambrilla de Castrejon.

milestones.event: Senda del Oro introduced as the estate's everyday Roble label

wines.description: Fresh, fruity Roble from Bodegas Vizcarra with 5 months oak, showing bright cherry, strawberry and floral notes from the high-altitude Mambrilla de Castrejon estate.

wines.name: Vizcarra 15 Meses
wines.oak_regime: 15 months in French oak barriques

taste.summary: The estate's signature mid-tier Reserva: 15 months in French oak delivers dark cherry, violet and cedar with the defining freshness and acidity of Vizcarra's high-altitude Burgos terroir.

pairings.dish: Roast rack of lamb with wild herbs
pairings.why: Dark fruit and fresh acidity beautifully complement the herb-fragrant lamb rack.
Silky tannins and dark fruit match the marbled pork fat.
Creamy sheep's-milk cheese softens the wine's firm tannins.

history.summary: Vizcarra 15 Meses has become one of the most recognisable wines in the Burgos sector of Ribera del Duero, noted for its combination of concentration and freshness. The name simply refers to the 15-month barrel-ageing period in French oak.

milestones.event: First 15 Meses vintage released as Vizcarra's Reserva-tier label

wines.description: Vizcarra's signature Reserva tier from high-altitude Mambrilla de Castrejon, aged 15 months in French oak showing dark cherry, violet, cedar and the estate's hallmark fresh acidity.

wines.name: Vizcarra Ines
wines.oak_regime: 20 months in new French oak

taste.summary: Vizcarra's prestige old-vine parcel wine, named for the estate founder's grandmother. Dense black fruit, graphite and espresso from 20 months in new French oak, with exceptional freshness for this concentration level.

pairings.why: Ultra-fine tannins and concentrated fruit call for the fat and char of premium beef.
Earthy graphite and black fruit suit the gamey venison with aromatic juniper.
Espresso and graphite notes echo the truffle's earthy depth.

history.summary: Ines is Vizcarra's homage to the estate founder's grandmother, produced from a single parcel of ancient Tinto Fino vines at Mambrilla de Castrejon. Only made in the best vintages and released after 20 months in new French oak and extended bottle rest.

milestones.event: First Ines vintage produced from the single old-vine parcel at Mambrilla

wines.description: Vizcarra's prestige old-vine single-parcel wine named for the founder's grandmother, aged 20 months in new French oak with concentrated black fruit, graphite and espresso and exceptional acidity.

wines.name: Astrales
wines.oak_regime: 16 months in French oak barriques, mostly new

taste.summary: The flagship from the tiny La Aguilera estate, combining concentration with freshness: violet, dark cherry and cedar from old-vine Tinto Fino aged 16 months in mostly-new French oak, with fine silky tannins.

pairings.why: Classic match -- Castilian roast suckling lamb and an old-vine La Aguilera red.
Silky tannins and dark fruit complement the premium pork.
Fine tannins meet the nutty, saline cheese to create a harmonious pairing.

history.summary: Astrales was established in 1998 and released its first vintage in 2000 from old-vine Tinto Fino parcels around La Aguilera in the Burgos sector. The estate focuses on a single main wine from a single vineyard, applying careful cellar work to reflect the terroir's elegance.

milestones.event: Astrales estate founded at La Aguilera, Burgos
First Astrales vintage released

wines.description: The flagship single-vineyard Tinto Fino from the tiny La Aguilera estate in Burgos, aged 16 months in mostly-new French oak with violet, dark cherry and silky, concentrated elegance.

wines.name: Christina
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French oak, mixed new and second-fill

taste.summary: The second wine from Astrales, sharing the estate's elegant, fresh profile: red cherry, plum and floral notes from 12 months in mixed-fill French oak, with bright acidity and silky tannins.

pairings.why: Medium body and fresh acidity are perfectly matched to well-seasoned roast chicken.
The wine's fresh cherry and floral character complements the clean pork flavour.
Bright fruit and medium tannins make a fine partner for saffron-scented rice.

history.summary: Christina is the second label from Astrales at La Aguilera, named for a family member of the founding owners. It shares the estate's vineyard but applies a lighter touch with shorter and less intensive oak ageing, offering the Astrales house style at a more accessible price.

milestones.event: First Christina vintage released as Astrales second wine

wines.description: Astrales's accessible second wine from La Aguilera in Burgos, aged 12 months in mixed French oak with fresh red cherry, floral notes and bright acidity in an elegant lighter register.

wines.name: Teofilo Reyes Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in American and French oak

taste.summary: A classic Ribera Crianza bearing the founding winemaker's name: ripe plum, cherry and American-oak coconut vanilla in a medium-bodied, food-friendly style.

pairings.why: American oak vanilla and cherry fruit complement the mild, fatty Castilian lamb.
Coconut vanilla from oak mirrors the sweet glaze and matches pork fat.
Hearty Castilian dish pairs naturally with this rustic, everyday-style Crianza.

history.summary: Bodegas Reyes bears the name of its founding winemaker Teofilo Reyes, who was a pioneer in the Pedrosa de Duero area and a formative influence on the appellation's quality identity in the 1980s and early 1990s. The Crianza carries his name as a lasting tribute.

milestones.event: First Teofilo Reyes Crianza vintage released under the estate name

wines.description: Classic Pedrosa de Duero Crianza bearing founding winemaker Teofilo Reyes's name, with ripe plum, cherry and American-oak vanilla in a medium-bodied, traditional style.

wines.name: Teofilo Reyes Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 20 months in American and French oak

taste.summary: Traditional extended-oak Reserva from Bodegas Reyes: dark plum, tobacco and vanilla from 20 months in mixed-species oak, with earthy mineral notes reflecting the Pedrosa de Duero clay-limestone terroir.

pairings.why: Firm tannins, dark fruit and cedar match the richness of Castilian roast suckling pig.
Structured wine needs the richness and smokiness of the paprika-basted steak.
The wine's firm tannins are softened by the creamy, pungent sheep's-milk cheese.

history.summary: The Teofilo Reyes Reserva follows the traditional style of the founding winemaker, with extended oak ageing in a mix of American and French barrels that was typical of the early-quality Ribera del Duero era.

milestones.event: First Teofilo Reyes Reserva released from Pedrosa de Duero

wines.description: Traditional Reserva from Bodegas Reyes at Pedrosa de Duero, aged 20 months in mixed oak with dark plum, tobacco and vanilla in the old-school Ribera style of founding winemaker Teofilo Reyes.

wines.name: Teofilo Reyes Gran Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 24 months in French oak followed by 36 months bottle ageing

taste.summary: Mature, complex Gran Reserva from Bodegas Reyes, requiring patience but rewarding with leather, tobacco and balsamic complexity from 24 months oak and 36 months minimum in bottle.

pairings.dish: Roast suckling lamb with garlic and herbs
pairings.why: The wine's mature, complex character needs a classic Castilian roast at table.

pairings.dish: Game bird with mushroom and truffle sauce
pairings.why: Leather, tobacco and balsamic notes find their echo in the gamey bird and earthy sauce.
Mature tannins and dried-fruit character pair with medium-aged cured sheep's cheese.

history.summary: The Gran Reserva from Bodegas Reyes represents the peak of the estate's traditional ageing philosophy: 24 months in French oak and a minimum 36 months in bottle before release. Only made in exceptional vintages, this is the Pedrosa de Duero style at its most traditional.

milestones.event: First Teofilo Reyes Gran Reserva released after extended cellar ageing

wines.description: Bodegas Reyes's apex Gran Reserva from Pedrosa de Duero aged 24 months in oak and 36 months in bottle, offering mature leather, tobacco and balsamic complexity in the traditional Ribera style.

wines.name: Pradorey Elite
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in new French oak barriques

taste.summary: Pradorey's flagship Reserva from La Horra, showing concentrated dark cherry, cassis and violet with cedar and a mineral backbone from 18 months in new French oak in the estate's modern gravity-flow cellar.

pairings.why: Concentrated dark fruit and fine tannins are a natural match for herb-crusted rack of lamb.
The wine's concentration and cedar spice suit the premium marbled pork cut.
Dark fruit and mineral backbone pair elegantly with the cheese-and-quince combination.

history.summary: Pradorey Elite is the flagship red from the La Horra estate, processed in a 21st-century gravity-flow cellar and aged 18 months in new French oak. The La Horra terroir -- calcareous clay at altitude -- produces fruit of depth and concentration.

milestones.event: First Pradorey Elite vintage released from the La Horra estate

wines.description: Pradorey's flagship Reserva from La Horra, aged 18 months in new French oak in a modern gravity-flow cellar, with concentrated dark cherry, cassis and a mineral backbone.

wines.name: Pradorey Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in French oak

taste.summary: The accessible Reserva tier from Pradorey at La Horra, with plum, dark cherry and sweet spice from 14 months in French oak; smooth and well-proportioned from the estate's gravity-fed cellar.

pairings.why: Smooth tannins and dark fruit complement the rich, slow-cooked lamb.
The wine's balance and sweet spice pair well with smoky, slow-cooked ribs.
Everyday hearty Castilian dish pairs comfortably with this well-structured Reserva.

history.summary: Pradorey Reserva is the entry to the estate's Reserva range at La Horra, spending 14 months in French oak before bottle rest. Approachable relative to the flagship Elite, it introduces the estate's La Horra calcareous terroir at a more accessible price.

milestones.event: First Pradorey Reserva vintage released

wines.description: Accessible Reserva from Pradorey's La Horra estate, aged 14 months in French oak with plum, dark cherry and sweet spice in a smooth, well-proportioned style.

wines.name: Pradorey Finca Valdelayegua
wines.oak_regime: 24 months in new French oak

taste.summary: Pradorey's prestige single-vineyard expression from the Valdelayegua parcel: cassis, violet and incense with graphite and licorice depth from 24 months in new French oak.

pairings.why: High concentration and dense tannins demand the richness and fat of properly aged beef.
Graphite, incense and concentrated fruit mirror earthy game and forest mushrooms.
The wine's fine tannins and concentration are grounded by crystalline aged cheese.

history.summary: Finca Valdelayegua is Pradorey's single-parcel prestige wine, from a specific old-vine plot at La Horra. It represents the estate's statement of maximum terroir expression, aged 24 months in new French oak and released only in the finest vintages.

milestones.event: First Finca Valdelayegua vintage released as Pradorey's prestige parcel wine

wines.description: Pradorey's prestige single-parcel old-vine Tinto Fino from the Valdelayegua plot at La Horra, aged 24 months in new French oak with cassis, violet, graphite and licorice depth.

wines.name: Aster Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: Classic Ribera Crianza from the La Rioja Alta group's Anguix estate: red cherry, plum and cedar with earthy spice in a balanced, medium-bodied style reflecting the long-oak-ageing philosophy of the Rioja parent.

pairings.why: Classic Castilian pairing for this traditionally styled Crianza.
Cedar and herb notes in the wine complement the aromatic pork.
Everyday Castilian comfort food meets the wine's earthy, accessible style.

history.summary: Bodegas Aster was established in 2000 as the Ribera del Duero arm of La Rioja Alta group, based at Anguix in the eastern Burgos sector. The estate applies the Rioja parent's philosophy of long barrel and bottle ageing to the region's Tinto Fino.

milestones.event: Bodegas Aster established by La Rioja Alta group at Anguix
First Aster Crianza vintage released

wines.description: Classic Ribera Crianza from La Rioja Alta's Anguix estate, with red cherry, plum and cedar in a balanced, medium-bodied style that echoes the Rioja parent's long-ageing philosophy.

wines.name: Aster Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 24 months in French oak, second and third-fill

taste.summary: The extended-oak Reserva from Aster spends 24 months in older French barrels following the La Rioja Alta philosophy, developing dark plum, tobacco, cedar and earthy mineral complexity.

pairings.why: The wine's firm structure and tobacco-cedar character suit the rich, crispy pork.
The wine's earthy, licorice notes mirror the slow-cooked gelatinous oxtail.
Hard, savoury sheep's-milk cheese pairs with firm tannins and cedar spice.

history.summary: Aster Reserva applies the La Rioja Alta group's signature 24-month barrel ageing to Ribera's Tinto Fino, using older French barrels to integrate oak rather than dominate fruit. The eastern Burgos terroir at Anguix provides additional mineral complexity.

milestones.event: First Aster Reserva vintage released

wines.description: Extended-oak Reserva from Aster at Anguix, aged 24 months in older French barrels following La Rioja Alta philosophy, with dark plum, tobacco, cedar and earthy mineral complexity.

wines.name: Aster Finca El Otero
wines.oak_regime: 20 months in new and second-fill French oak

taste.summary: Aster's prestige single-vineyard label from the El Otero parcel, with concentrated cassis, graphite and mineral character from 20 months in French oak and the estate's best old vines.

pairings.dish: Roast rack of lamb with garlic and thyme
pairings.why: The wine's polished intensity and cedar spice suit aromatic herb-roasted lamb.
Polished tannins and concentrated fruit complement the premium marbled Iberico cut.
Firm tannins and mineral notes pair well with the firm savoury sheep's cheese.

history.summary: Finca El Otero is Aster's prestige single-parcel selection from the estate's best plots at Anguix. It represents the La Rioja Alta group's ambition for a Ribera del Duero wine of the highest quality, using the long ageing and careful barrel philosophy they apply in Rioja.

milestones.event: First Finca El Otero vintage released as Aster's prestige label

wines.description: Aster's prestige El Otero parcel selection from Anguix, aged 20 months in French oak with concentrated cassis, graphite and mineral precision from the La Rioja Alta group's Ribera estate.

wines.name: Urban Ribera
wines.oak_regime: 8 months in second-fill French oak

taste.summary: O Fournier's fresh, accessible entry label from Berlangas de Roa: bright cherry, red berry and light vanilla from brief French oak contact, designed for early enjoyment.

pairings.why: Versatile, fruity style suits a range of small plates.
Fresh fruit and light tannins complement the smoky, paprika-seasoned bird.
Bright acidity and cherry fruit pair well with the crispy, sauced potatoes.

history.summary: Urban Ribera is the everyday entry label from Bodegas O Fournier at Berlangas de Roa, designed for approachable Tinto Fino drinking. The Fournier family group established their Ribera estate in 2000 to complement their Argentine and Chilean operations.

milestones.event: Bodegas O Fournier established at Berlangas de Roa
First Urban Ribera vintage released

wines.description: O Fournier's approachable entry label from the Berlangas de Roa estate, showing fresh cherry, red berry and light vanilla from brief oak contact for everyday Ribera drinking.

wines.name: Alfa Spiga
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak barriques

taste.summary: O Fournier's prestige Reserva from the Burgos sector: concentrated dark fruit, cedar and graphite from 18 months in French oak, showing the ambition of the Fournier family's Ribera project.

pairings.why: Concentrated fruit and cedar suit herb-fragrant slow-roasted lamb.
Dense tannins need the fat and depth of premium Iberico.
Firm structure and dark fruit are complemented by pungent aged cheese.

history.summary: Alfa Spiga is the prestige label from Bodegas O Fournier at Berlangas de Roa, drawing on the estate's best Tinto Fino parcels in the Burgos sector. The Fournier family, with roots in Argentina and Chile, applied international expertise to this Ribera project.

milestones.event: First Alfa Spiga vintage released from Berlangas de Roa estate

wines.description: O Fournier's prestige Reserva from Berlangas de Roa, aged 18 months in French oak with concentrated dark fruit, cedar and graphite from the Burgos-sector Tinto Fino parcels.

wines.name: Spiga
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French oak

taste.summary: Mid-range Reserva from O Fournier with cherry, plum and dried herbs balanced by light cedar from 12 months in French oak; accessible and approachable from the Berlangas de Roa estate.

pairings.why: The wine's medium body and red fruit are a classic match for lamb.
Dried herb notes echo the seasoning and the wine's medium body balances the pork.
Red fruit and cedar are versatile partners to charcuterie and medium cheese.

history.summary: Spiga is the mid-tier label from Bodegas O Fournier at Berlangas de Roa, positioned between the entry Urban Ribera and the prestige Alfa Spiga. It applies a 12-month French oak regime to Tinto Fino from the Burgos sector parcels.

milestones.event: First Spiga vintage released as O Fournier's mid-tier label

wines.description: O Fournier's mid-tier Ribera label from Berlangas de Roa with cherry, plum and dried herbs from 12 months in French oak in an accessible, approachable style.

wines.name: Convento de Oreja Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: Approachable Crianza from the Peñafiel-area Convento de Oreja estate: fresh cherry, plum and vanilla from 12 months in mixed oak, with balanced light tannins suited to everyday drinking.

pairings.why: Medium-bodied red fruit is a natural match for well-seasoned chicken.
The wine's red fruit and moderate tannins suit pork sausages off the grill.
Fresh cherry and balanced acidity pair well with the eggy Spanish omelette.

history.summary: Convento de Oreja is named for the historic convent that once occupied the Peñafiel-area estate site. The Crianza showcases accessible, well-balanced Tinto Fino from the Valladolid valley floor at an everyday price.

milestones.event: First Convento de Oreja Crianza released from Peñafiel area estate

wines.description: Accessible Tinto Fino Crianza from the Convento de Oreja estate near Peñafiel, with fresh cherry, plum and vanilla from 12 months mixed oak for everyday Ribera drinking.

wines.name: Convento de Oreja Seleccion
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak

taste.summary: The prestige selection from Convento de Oreja: concentrated dark cherry, blackberry and cedar from 18 months in French oak, more structured and age-worthy than the standard Crianza.

pairings.why: The wine's concentrated dark fruit and structured tannins suit slow-cooked rich lamb.

pairings.dish: Beef casserole with tomato and herbs
pairings.why: Earthy herbs and cedar in the wine match hearty Castilian beef stew.
Firm tannins and dark fruit balance medium-aged sheep's cheese.

history.summary: The Seleccion tier from Convento de Oreja is a step up from the standard Crianza, drawing from the estate's best old-vine parcels near Peñafiel and spending 18 months in French oak. Made only in selected vintages.

milestones.event: First Convento de Oreja Seleccion released from old-vine parcel selection

wines.description: The prestige parcel selection from Convento de Oreja near Peñafiel, aged 18 months in French oak with concentrated dark cherry, blackberry and cedar in a structured, age-worthy style.

wines.name: Tarsus Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in French oak

taste.summary: Polished Crianza from Tarsus at La Horra with cherry, plum and violet from 14 months in French oak; smooth, structured tannins and good length from the high-altitude Burgos-sector vineyards.

pairings.why: Classic Ribera pairing -- cherry and violet in the wine complement the lamb's mild fat.
Structured tannins and dark fruit suit the smoky, marbled pork chop.
Polished tannins and cherry fruit pair with the nutty aged cheese.

history.summary: Bodegas Tarsus was founded in 1994 at La Horra in the Burgos sector, producing its first Crianza in 1996. The estate has developed a reputation for structured, consistently age-worthy Tinto Fino from the high-altitude limestone terroir.

milestones.event: Bodegas Tarsus founded at La Horra
First Tarsus Crianza vintage released

wines.description: Polished Tinto Fino Crianza from the La Horra estate Tarsus, aged 14 months in French oak with cherry, violet and smooth cedar tannins from high-altitude Burgos terroir.

wines.name: Tarsus Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak

taste.summary: The Tarsus Reserva adds weight and complexity to the estate's style: dark cherry, cassis, licorice and cedar from 18 months in French oak, with firm, age-worthy tannins from La Horra limestone.

pairings.why: Firm tannins and concentrated dark fruit anchor the richness of crispy cochinillo.

pairings.dish: Braised veal cheeks in red wine
pairings.why: The wine's licorice and cedar notes merge with the slow-cooked gelatinous sauce.
Firm, savoury cheese balances the concentrated dark fruit and tannins.

history.summary: The Tarsus Reserva builds on the La Horra estate's strengths: extended 18-month oak ageing adds structure and complexity to the naturally concentrated fruit from the high-altitude limestone vineyards. It has become the estate's most recognised label.

milestones.event: First Tarsus Reserva vintage released from La Horra estate

wines.description: Tarsus Reserva from La Horra in Burgos sector, aged 18 months in French oak with dark cherry, cassis, licorice and cedar; firm, age-worthy tannins from high-altitude limestone terroir.

wines.name: Tarsus Quintus
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new French oak

taste.summary: The prestige Quintus label from Tarsus: concentrated cassis, violet and graphite from new French oak and old vines at La Horra, with polished, age-worthy tannins at the highest tier.

pairings.why: Dense tannins and graphite require the fat and depth of properly aged premium beef.
Graphite and espresso notes in the wine echo the earthy mushroom and truffle.

pairings.dish: Manchego DOP viejo with quince paste
pairings.why: Concentrated fruit and firm structure are matched by aged cheese and sweet quince.

history.summary: Quintus is Tarsus's apex label, named in the Roman tradition and representing the estate's finest selection from La Horra. Aged 22 months in new French oak and released only in the best years, it represents the Burgos sector at its most concentrated.

milestones.event: First Tarsus Quintus vintage released as the estate's prestige label

wines.description: Tarsus's prestige Quintus from old vines at La Horra, aged 22 months in new French oak with concentrated cassis, violet, graphite and espresso; only made in the best years.

wines.name: Vatan Ribera del Duero
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak, mixed new and used

taste.summary: A single-wine estate from La Horra producing an extractive, concentrated Tinto Fino with dark cherry, blackcurrant, herbal notes and a mineral backbone from the calcareous clay soils of the Burgos plateau.

pairings.why: Concentrated dark fruit and firm structure match the full-flavoured lechazo.
Dense tannins and dark fruit suit the fat and smokiness of premium Iberico.
The wine's firm mineral tannins pair with the crystalline texture of viejo Manchego.

history.summary: Bodegas Vatan is a small La Horra estate producing a single prestige Tinto Fino from old-vine parcels on calcareous clay soils. The estate's reputation for extractive, age-worthy style has made it sought-after among Ribera collectors.

milestones.event: First Vatan Ribera del Duero vintage released from La Horra old vines

wines.description: Single-wine estate from La Horra producing extractive, concentrated old-vine Tinto Fino with dark cherry, mineral backbone and firm tannins suited to long cellaring.

wines.name: Hacienda Zorita Natural Reserve
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in French oak

taste.summary: Organic VT Castilla y Leon from the Salamanca province estate: ripe cherry, blackberry and herbs from Tinto Fino and Cabernet Sauvignon, with 14 months French oak and the organic estate's clean, fresh profile.

pairings.dish: Roast chicken with pimentón and herbs
pairings.why: Medium body and red fruit complement the smoky herb-roasted chicken.
The wine's fruit and moderate tannins suit the clean pork flavour.
Everyday earthiness and red fruit match the warm spiced lentil dish.

history.summary: Hacienda Zorita's Natural Reserve is produced from the organic estate in Salamanca province classified as VT Castilla y Leon, outside the Ribera del Duero DO boundary. The estate pairs wine production with ecological tourism and an organic farmstead.

milestones.event: First Natural Reserve vintage from the organic Hacienda Zorita estate

wines.description: Organic VT Castilla y Leon blend from Hacienda Zorita's Salamanca estate, with cherry, blackberry and herbs from Tinto Fino and Cabernet Sauvignon aged 14 months in French oak.

wines.name: Hacienda Zorita Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak

taste.summary: The Reserva tier from Hacienda Zorita's organic Salamanca estate: concentrated dark plum, blackberry and cedar from Tinto Fino, Cabernet Sauvignon and Syrah aged 18 months in French oak.

pairings.why: Firm structure and dark fruit suit the rich, slow-cooked lamb.
Concentrated dark fruit and structured tannins need the richness of beef steak.
Firm tannins and dark fruit are balanced by the nutty, sweet cheese pairing.

history.summary: The Hacienda Zorita Reserva represents the extended-ageing tier from the organic Salamanca province estate, blending native Tinto Fino with Cabernet Sauvignon and Syrah and spending 18 months in French oak for a fuller, more complex expression.

milestones.event: First Hacienda Zorita Reserva released from the organic Salamanca estate

wines.description: Organic VT Castilla y Leon Reserva from Hacienda Zorita's Salamanca estate, blending Tinto Fino, Cabernet Sauvignon and Syrah aged 18 months in French oak with dark plum, cedar and structured tannins.

wines.name: Vizcarra Ramos Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: Approachable Crianza from Bodegas Vizcarra Ramos at Fuentelcesped on the eastern Burgos plateau, with cherry, plum and cedar from 14 months in mixed oak and balanced, food-friendly tannins.

pairings.why: Classic Ribera pairing of red fruit and medium tannins with mild lamb.
The wine's spice and moderate tannins complement pork in pepper sauce.
Fresh cherry and light cedar match the saline depth of Iberico jamon.

history.summary: Bodegas Vizcarra Ramos operates from the clay-limestone soils near Fuentelcesped east of Aranda de Duero, a different estate from the related Bodegas Vizcarra at Mambrilla de Castrejon. The Crianza reflects the concentrated style typical of the eastern Burgos sub-zone.

milestones.event: First Vizcarra Ramos Crianza released from Fuentelcesped estate

wines.description: Approachable Tinto Fino Crianza from Bodegas Vizcarra Ramos at Fuentelcesped in the eastern Burgos sector, with cherry, plum and cedar from 14 months in mixed oak.

wines.name: Vizcarra Ramos Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 20 months in French oak

taste.summary: Structured Reserva from the Fuentelcesped estate: dark plum, blackcurrant and tobacco from 20 months in French oak, with earthy tannins and mineral notes reflecting the clay-limestone soils of the eastern Burgos plateau.

pairings.why: Firm tannins and concentrated dark fruit balance the rich cochinillo.
The wine's earthy mineral notes merge with hearty Castilian beef stew.
Firm, food-friendly tannins pair with saline aged Manchego or Zamorano.

history.summary: Vizcarra Ramos Reserva extends the oak regime to 20 months in French barrels, drawing from the estate's best parcels near Fuentelcesped. The eastern Burgos sub-zone's clay-limestone soils at altitude support concentrated, mineral Tinto Fino suited to the Reserva regime.

milestones.event: First Vizcarra Ramos Reserva released from Fuentelcesped

wines.description: Structured Reserva from Bodegas Vizcarra Ramos at Fuentelcesped, aged 20 months in French oak with dark plum, tobacco, cedar and earthy mineral notes from the clay-limestone plateau soils.

wines.name: Vizcarra Ramos Torralvo
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new and used French oak

taste.summary: Torralvo is the prestige old-vine single-parcel selection from Vizcarra Ramos, with concentrated cassis, violet, graphite and cedar from 22 months in oak and the estate's oldest vines.

pairings.dish: Prime Castilian beef chop on woodfire
pairings.why: High concentration and graphite need the char and fat of prime woodfire beef.
Violet and incense notes in the wine mirror the gamey depth of slow venison.
Crystalline old cheese provides the saline counterpoint to concentrated fruit.

history.summary: Torralvo is the parcel-selection prestige wine from Bodegas Vizcarra Ramos at Fuentelcesped, named for the specific vineyard site. It is produced from the estate's oldest Tinto Fino vines and made only in exceptional vintages.

milestones.event: First Torralvo vintage released from old-vine Fuentelcesped parcel

wines.description: Vizcarra Ramos's prestige old-vine parcel wine from Fuentelcesped, aged 22 months in French oak with concentrated cassis, graphite and violet; made only in exceptional years.

wines.name: Leda Vinas Viejas
wines.oak_regime: 16 months in French oak barriques

taste.summary: Leda Vinas Viejas sources ungrafted centenarian Tinto Fino vines from across the Castilian plateau, classified VT Castilla y Leon due to multi-province sourcing: concentrated dark fruit, mineral, cedar and high acidity from very old ungrafted rootstock.

pairings.why: The wine's concentration and mineral acidity are a natural match for slow-roasted lechazo.
Firm tannins and mineral precision are complemented by crystalline aged cheese.
The wine's fat-cutting acidity and dark fruit suit the marbled Iberico cut.

history.summary: Bodegas Leda sources pre-phylloxera ungrafted centenarian Tinto Fino vines from parcels scattered across the Castilian plateau. Because the vineyards span multiple provinces, the wine cannot carry the DO Ribera del Duero designation and is classified VT Castilla y Leon.

milestones.event: First Leda Vinas Viejas vintage released from multi-province ungrafted vines

wines.description: VT Castilla y Leon from ungrafted centenarian Tinto Fino vines across the Castilian plateau; concentrated dark fruit, mineral and high acidity from very old ungrafted rootstock.

wines.name: Mas de Leda
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French oak, second-fill

taste.summary: The accessible second label from Leda, sharing the multi-province old-vine sourcing philosophy with less oak intensity: red cherry, plum and light cedar with smooth tannins and balanced acidity.

pairings.why: Medium-bodied red fruit is an accessible partner for well-seasoned roast chicken.
Red fruit and light cedar match the romesco-sauced pork.
Everyday smoothness and balanced acidity pair with hearty Castilian vegetable stew.

history.summary: Mas de Leda is the second label from Bodegas Leda, sharing the same multi-province old-vine sourcing approach as the flagship Vinas Viejas but using a lighter oak regime and sourcing from younger old-vine parcels.

milestones.event: First Mas de Leda vintage released as Leda's accessible second wine

wines.description: Leda's accessible second label from multi-province old-vine Tinto Fino, aged 12 months in second-fill French oak with red cherry, plum and light cedar in a smooth, balanced style.

wines.name: Rolland and Galarreta
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in new and used French oak barriques

taste.summary: The single wine from the Michel Rolland and Inigo Galarreta project at La Horra: concentrated dark cherry, cassis and cedar from old Tinto Fino vines, shaped by Rolland's hallmark smooth-tannin approach.

pairings.why: Smooth concentrated tannins and dark fruit are a classic complement to slow-roasted lamb.
Rolland's polished style and the wine's concentration suit the marbled Iberico cut.
Smooth dark fruit and mineral notes pair with aged cheese and aromatic honey.

history.summary: The Rolland and Galarreta project was founded in 2005 as a collaboration between Bordeaux consultant Michel Rolland and Spanish partner Inigo Galarreta. Working from old-vine Tinto Fino parcels at La Horra, Rolland applies his signature smooth-tannin Bordeaux philosophy to the Burgos sector terroir.

milestones.event: Rolland and Galarreta project founded at La Horra
First Rolland and Galarreta vintage released from La Horra old vines

wines.description: The single wine from Michel Rolland and Inigo Galarreta's La Horra project, aged 18 months in French oak with concentrated dark cherry, cassis and cedar shaped by Rolland's smooth-tannin winemaking philosophy.

wines.name: Alma del Duero
wines.oak_regime: 10 months in used French oak

taste.summary: From Bodegas Mas Que Vinos's biodynamic-practicing estate at Valbuena de Duero: fresh cherry, earthy mineral and dried herbs from Tinto Fino aged briefly in used French oak with the estate's characteristic high acidity and low-intervention philosophy.

pairings.why: High acidity and fresh cherry pair well with smoky charred vegetables.
Earthy herbs and fresh fruit match the aromatic, lightly roasted bird.
Fresh acidity and mineral notes are versatile companions to light charcuterie.

history.summary: Alma del Duero is a Ribera del Duero label from the biodynamic-practicing Bodegas Mas Que Vinos estate at Valbuena de Duero, produced alongside their Ercavio range with a natural wine philosophy emphasising minimal intervention and low sulfur additions.

milestones.event: First Alma del Duero vintage produced under the natural wine philosophy

wines.description: Low-intervention Ribera del Duero from the biodynamic-practicing Mas Que Vinos estate at Valbuena de Duero, showing fresh cherry, earthy mineral and dried herbs with high acidity.

wines.name: Ercavio Tempranillo
wines.oak_regime: 6 months in used French oak

taste.summary: A VT Castilla natural-wine style from Mas Que Vinos with fresh cherry, raspberry and earthy mineral character; brief used-oak contact preserves the vivid fruit and natural freshness.

pairings.why: Versatile, fresh and mineral; suited to a wide range of lightly flavoured tapas.

pairings.dish: Lightly dressed salad with goats cheese
pairings.why: Racy acidity and fresh fruit are ideal with soft cheese and salad.
Refreshing acidity cuts through the fat of sliced jamon on tomato-rubbed bread.

history.summary: Ercavio is the natural-wine label from Bodegas Mas Que Vinos, classified as VT Castilla due to multi-province sourcing. The estate at Valbuena de Duero practices biodynamic farming and applies minimal intervention cellar work.

milestones.event: First Ercavio Tempranillo vintage released under the natural wine philosophy

wines.description: VT Castilla natural-wine label from Mas Que Vinos's biodynamic estate at Valbuena de Duero; fresh cherry and raspberry with earthy mineral character and racy acidity from minimal-intervention winemaking.

wines.name: Bodegas Roa Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: Approachable Crianza from the historic Roa de Duero town estate: cherry, plum and vanilla from 12 months in mixed oak, offering the limestone-chalk plateau terroir in a food-friendly everyday style.

pairings.why: Medium-bodied red fruit and balanced tannins pair with a simple garlic chicken.
The wine's everyday Crianza style suits hearty grilled sausage dishes.
Fresh cherry and light cedar match the mild freshness of young Manchego.

history.summary: Bodegas Roa is anchored to the historic town of Roa de Duero on the limestone-chalk plateau in Burgos province, producing wines that reflect the classic terroir of the western Burgos sector. The Crianza is the estate's standard-bearing everyday label.

milestones.event: First Bodegas Roa Crianza released from Roa de Duero limestone estate

wines.description: Approachable Tinto Fino Crianza from Bodegas Roa at the historic limestone-chalk Roa de Duero estate, with cherry, plum and vanilla from 12 months in mixed oak.

wines.name: Bodegas Roa Costera
wines.oak_regime: 4 months in American oak

taste.summary: Fresh, early-drinking Roble/Joven from Bodegas Roa with brief American oak, showing bright cherry, raspberry and floral notes for everyday bar-style drinking.

pairings.why: Fresh, fruity style is ideal for casual tapas settings.
Light tannins and fresh acidity complement the saline marinated anchovies.
High acidity and fresh fruit match charred vegetable skewers.

history.summary: Costera is the fresh, everyday entry label from Bodegas Roa, designed for bar culture and everyday casual enjoyment. Minimal oak contact preserves the bright cherry and raspberry freshness of young Tinto Fino from the Roa de Duero limestone plateau.

milestones.event: Costera introduced as Bodegas Roa's everyday bar-style label

wines.description: Fresh, easy-drinking Roble/Joven from Bodegas Roa with brief American oak, showing bright cherry, raspberry and floral notes for everyday casual enjoyment.

wines.name: Bodegas Roa Sylis
wines.oak_regime: 20 months in new and used French oak

taste.summary: The prestige label from Bodegas Roa, Sylis, draws from old Tinto Fino vines on the Roa plateau: concentrated dark cherry, cassis and tobacco from 20 months in French oak, with the mineral thread typical of the chalk-limestone soils.

pairings.why: Concentrated dark fruit and firm tannins are a natural pairing for slow-cooked lechazo.
The wine's concentration and cedar spice call for the fat and depth of a well-aged ribeye.
The mineral thread and concentrated fruit pair elegantly with hard aged cheese.

history.summary: Sylis is the Bodegas Roa prestige selection from old-vine Tinto Fino parcels on the Roa de Duero limestone-chalk plateau. The name references the estate's ancient Roman heritage as Roa de Duero was once a key Roman settlement in the Duero valley.

milestones.event: First Sylis vintage released as Bodegas Roa's prestige label

wines.description: Bodegas Roa's prestige old-vine selection from the Roa de Duero limestone-chalk plateau, aged 20 months in French oak with concentrated dark cherry, cassis, tobacco and mineral backbone.

wines.name: Condado de Haza Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in American oak

taste.summary: Classic Alejandro Fernandez-style Crianza from his second Ribera estate at La Horra: ripe cherry, plum and American-oak coconut vanilla in the traditional mould of the Pesquera school.

pairings.why: The classic Fernandez house style in American oak is a natural companion to slow Castilian roast lamb.
American vanilla oak and cherry fruit complement the smoky spiced pork.
Smooth tannins and red cherry match medium-aged sheep's-milk cheese.

history.summary: Condado de Haza was founded in 1989 by Alejandro Fernandez of Tinto Pesquera fame as his second Ribera del Duero estate at La Horra. It carries forward the same 100% Tinto Fino philosophy and American-oak ageing approach that Fernandez used at Pesquera to build the appellation's international reputation.

milestones.event: Condado de Haza founded by Alejandro Fernandez at La Horra
First Condado de Haza Crianza vintage released

wines.description: Classic Alejandro Fernandez-style Tinto Fino Crianza from Condado de Haza at La Horra, aged 12 months in American oak with cherry, plum and coconut vanilla in the traditional Pesquera school style.

wines.name: Alenza Gran Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 24 months in French and American oak, followed by 36 months bottle ageing

taste.summary: Alenza is the prestige Gran Reserva from Condado de Haza: mature plum, leather, tobacco and cedar spice from 24 months in mixed-species oak and 36 months minimum bottle ageing at the La Horra estate.

pairings.dish: Prime Castilian beef with bone marrow
pairings.why: Mature, complex tannins and leather need the fat and richness of aged prime beef.
Leather and tobacco notes in the wine complement gamey birds with aromatic juniper.
The wine's mature complexity pairs elegantly with crystalline aged cheese and sweet quince.

history.summary: Alenza Gran Reserva is the flagship label from Condado de Haza, applying Alejandro Fernandez's traditional long-ageing approach: 24 months in oak and a minimum of 36 months in bottle before release. Made only in exceptional years from the best old-vine La Horra parcels.

milestones.event: First Alenza Gran Reserva released from Condado de Haza's best La Horra parcels

wines.description: Condado de Haza's prestige Gran Reserva from La Horra, aged 24 months in oak and minimum 36 months in bottle with mature plum, leather, tobacco and balsamic complexity.

wines.name: Condado de Haza Roble
wines.oak_regime: 6 months in American oak

taste.summary: The accessible Roble from Alejandro Fernandez's Condado de Haza estate, with brief American oak showing fresh cherry, plum and coconut vanilla for early everyday drinking.

pairings.why: Fresh cherry and vanilla complement the saline fat of sliced Iberico ham.
Gentle tannins and fresh acidity pair well with the crispy spiced potato.
Accessible fruit and low tannins match simple grilled poultry.

history.summary: Condado de Haza Roble is the estate's entry-level label, introducing the classic Alejandro Fernandez American-oak style at a more accessible tier. Short 6-month American oak contact adds coconut vanilla without losing the wine's fruit freshness.

milestones.event: First Condado de Haza Roble released as the estate entry label

wines.description: Entry-level Roble from Alejandro Fernandez's Condado de Haza estate at La Horra, with 6 months in American oak showing fresh cherry, plum and coconut vanilla for everyday drinking.

wines.name: Diez Llorente Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: A small family estate Crianza from the Valladolid valley floor at Pesquera de Duero: cherry, plum and vanilla from 14 months in mixed oak, focused on expressing the clay-limestone terroir in a clean, direct style.

pairings.why: Classic Ribera pairing -- red fruit and vanilla complement the aromatic lamb.
Smooth tannins and red fruit work well with simple herb-roasted pork.
Balanced everyday red with gentle tannins pairs well with a classic cheese and meat board.

history.summary: Bodegas Diez Llorente is a small family estate at Pesquera de Duero, focused on expressing the clay-limestone soils of the Valladolid valley floor through traditional Crianza ageing. The estate produces a focused range of two cuvees.

milestones.event: First Diez Llorente Crianza from Pesquera de Duero estate

wines.description: Small-family-estate Crianza from Bodegas Diez Llorente at Pesquera de Duero, with cherry, plum and vanilla from 14 months in mixed oak, focused on clean terroir expression from Valladolid clay-limestone.

wines.name: Garcia Figuero Roble
wines.oak_regime: 4 months in American oak

taste.summary: Fresh, fruit-forward Roble from Garcia Figuero's La Horra estate: bright cherry, strawberry and vanilla from 4 months in American oak, with high acidity and low tannins suited to immediate enjoyment.

pairings.why: Fresh acidity and light tannins suit a wide range of light snacks.
Light-bodied fruit matches the seasoned char of pork skewers.
The wine's fresh cherry and vanilla echoes the sweet-savoury jamon-melon classic.

history.summary: Garcia Figuero at La Horra has been a reliable La Horra sub-zone benchmark since the late 1990s. The Roble is the estate's entry fresh-drinking label, spending just 4 months in American oak to maintain the bright cherry and strawberry character of Tinto Fino at this altitude.

milestones.event: First Garcia Figuero Roble vintage released as the estate entry label

wines.description: Fresh entry-level Roble from Garcia Figuero's La Horra estate with 4 months American oak, showing bright cherry, strawberry and vanilla for immediate everyday enjoyment.

wines.name: Garcia Figuero Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: Garcia Figuero's benchmark Crianza from La Horra: cherry, plum and cedar from 12 months in mixed oak, offering a reliable expression of the La Horra sub-zone's calcareous clay soils.

pairings.why: Classic Ribera pairing -- the wine's cherry and cedar match mild Castilian lamb.
The wine's balance and medium body suit well-seasoned pork.
Smooth tannins and cherry fruit work well with medium-aged sheep's cheese.

history.summary: Garcia Figuero has been producing wines from La Horra since 1998 and the Crianza was one of the first labels released. Over the years it has become a reliable, widely-distributed benchmark for the La Horra sub-zone style.

milestones.event: Garcia Figuero estate established at La Horra
First Crianza vintage released

wines.description: Benchmark Tinto Fino Crianza from Garcia Figuero's La Horra estate, aged 12 months in mixed oak with cherry, plum and cedar in a reliably balanced, accessible style.

wines.name: Garcia Figuero Tinto 15 Meses
wines.oak_regime: 15 months in French oak barriques

taste.summary: Named for its 15-month French oak regime, this is Garcia Figuero's Reserva-tier: concentrated dark cherry, cassis, violet and cedar from selected old-vine La Horra parcels.

pairings.why: Concentrated dark fruit and firm structure suit the richness of slow Castilian lamb.

pairings.dish: Grilled ribeye with bone marrow butter
pairings.why: Firm tannins and concentration need the fat and depth of premium beef.
Cedar, violet and dark fruit pair with aged cheese and earthy truffle.

history.summary: Garcia Figuero's 15 Meses label occupies the mid-premium tier, spending exactly 15 months in French oak barriques from the estate's best La Horra parcels. It is positioned between the Crianza and the prestige Tinus single-vineyard.

milestones.event: First Garcia Figuero 15 Meses vintage released

wines.description: Garcia Figuero's Reserva-tier label aged 15 months in French oak, with concentrated dark cherry, cassis, violet and cedar from selected La Horra old-vine parcels.

wines.name: Tinus
wines.oak_regime: 20 months in new French oak barriques

taste.summary: Garcia Figuero's prestige single-vineyard selection: concentrated cassis, violet and graphite from the estate's best old-vine parcel at La Horra, aged 20 months in new French oak and released only in exceptional vintages.

pairings.why: Dense graphite tannins and concentrated fruit call for the fat and depth of prime beef.

pairings.dish: Venison haunch with red berry sauce
pairings.why: Violet and cassis in the wine mirror the gamey meat and fruity sauce.

pairings.dish: Aged Manchego DOP viejo with walnut
pairings.why: Ultra-fine tannins pair with the nutty, crystalline texture of old Manchego.

history.summary: Tinus is Garcia Figuero's apex label, produced from the single parcel with the oldest Tinto Fino vines at La Horra. The name references the Tinto Fino grape (also called Tinto del Pais or Tinta del Toro). Only made in exceptional vintages and aged 20 months in new French oak.

milestones.event: First Tinus vintage released from the Garcia Figuero best old-vine parcel

wines.description: Garcia Figuero's prestige old-vine single-parcel expression from La Horra, aged 20 months in new French oak with concentrated cassis, violet, graphite and espresso; released only in exceptional years.

wines.name: Milagros de Figuero
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in new and second-fill French oak

taste.summary: Milagros de Figuero is a second prestige expression from Garcia Figuero, drawing from a different old-vine parcel at La Horra: concentrated dark fruit, incense and cedar with polished tannins from 18 months in French oak.

pairings.why: Polished concentrated tannins and dark fruit are a natural companion for aromatic roast lamb.
The wine's concentration and cedar spice match slow-cooked richly braised ribs.
Dark fruit and polished tannins are lifted by the saline-sweet aged cheese and honey.

history.summary: Milagros de Figuero is Garcia Figuero's second prestige single-vineyard label, produced from a specifically selected old-vine parcel at La Horra separate from the Tinus vineyard. The name translates to 'Miracles of Figuero', reflecting the estate's pride in La Horra's natural endowment.

milestones.event: First Milagros de Figuero vintage from a distinct La Horra old-vine parcel

wines.description: Garcia Figuero's second prestige single-vineyard expression from a distinct La Horra old-vine parcel, aged 18 months in French oak with concentrated dark fruit, incense and polished cedar tannins.

wines.name: Alonso del Yerro
wines.oak_regime: 16 months in French oak barriques

taste.summary: The flagship from the Derenoncourt-consulted Alonso del Yerro estate at Quintanilla de Onesimo: dark cherry, violet and cedar with silky tannins and mineral precision from the Valladolid limestone soils.

pairings.why: Silky tannins and fresh dark fruit complement the aromatic herb-fragrant roast lamb.
The wine's precision and silky tannins suit the clean, lean Iberico pork cut.
Mineral precision and cedar spice are elevated by the smoky sheep's-milk cheese.

history.summary: Alonso del Yerro was founded in 2003 by the Alonso del Yerro family at Quintanilla de Onesimo with consulting input from Bordeaux consultant Stephane Derenoncourt. The flagship wine expresses the precision and freshness typical of the Valladolid sector limestone terroir.

milestones.event: Alonso del Yerro estate founded with Derenoncourt consulting
First Alonso del Yerro flagship vintage released

wines.description: The flagship from the Derenoncourt-consulted Alonso del Yerro estate at Quintanilla de Onesimo, aged 16 months in French oak with dark cherry, violet, silky tannins and mineral precision.

wines.name: Maria
wines.oak_regime: 20 months in new French oak

taste.summary: Maria is the prestige single-vineyard label from Alonso del Yerro: concentrated cassis, graphite and mineral precision from a single old-vine parcel at Quintanilla de Onesimo, aged 20 months in new French oak under Derenoncourt's guidance.

pairings.why: The wine's concentration and fine tannins call for the fat and depth of properly aged beef.

pairings.dish: Roast venison with black cherry sauce
pairings.why: Cassis and graphite notes mirror the gamey venison and fruit-forward reduction.
Graphite and mineral notes echo the truffle's earthy depth in the egg dish.

history.summary: Maria is Alonso del Yerro's prestige single-vineyard label, named for a family member of the founding owners and produced from the estate's single finest old-vine parcel. Stephane Derenoncourt's influence is evident in the Bordeaux-influenced precision and structural elegance.

milestones.event: First Maria vintage from the estate's best single-vineyard parcel

wines.description: Alonso del Yerro's prestige single-vineyard Maria from Quintanilla de Onesimo, aged 20 months in new French oak with concentrated cassis, graphite and mineral precision under Derenoncourt's guidance.

wines.name: Portia Prima
wines.oak_regime: 10 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: The accessible entry label from Foster + Partners-designed Portia winery at Gumiel de Izán: fresh cherry, plum and vanilla from 10 months in mixed oak, balanced and food-friendly.

pairings.why: Medium-bodied red fruit and balanced tannins pair with well-seasoned roast chicken.

pairings.dish: Pork loin with garlic and herbs
pairings.why: The wine's approachable style suits clean pork with aromatic herbs.
Fresh cherry and balanced acidity work well with young cheese and fresh tomato.

history.summary: Portia Prima is the entry label from Bodegas Portia at Gumiel de Izán, housed in the striking star-shaped winery designed by Norman Foster's Foster + Partners practice and opened in 2010. The wine is made to be accessible and food-friendly for the estate's many wine-tourism visitors.

milestones.event: Foster + Partners-designed Portia winery opens at Gumiel de Izán
First Portia Prima vintage released

wines.description: Accessible entry-level Tinto Fino from the architecturally iconic Portia winery at Gumiel de Izán, aged 10 months in mixed oak with fresh cherry, plum and vanilla.

wines.name: Portia Ebeia
wines.oak_regime: 16 months in French oak

taste.summary: Portia's mid-premium Reserva: concentrated dark cherry, cassis and cedar from 16 months in French oak, showing the Gumiel de Izán terroir in a more structured, age-worthy style than the entry Prima.

pairings.why: Firm structure and concentrated dark fruit balance the richness of Castilian cochinillo.

pairings.dish: Grilled lamb ribs with garlic aioli
pairings.why: Cedar and dark fruit match lamb ribs and the richness of aioli.
Firm tannins and dark fruit pair with medium-cured sheep's cheese.

history.summary: Ebeia is Portia's Reserva tier, named for the ancient Iberian settlement near Gumiel de Izán. It spends 16 months in French oak and represents the estate's expression of greater concentration and structure from its best Tinto Fino parcels.

milestones.event: First Portia Ebeia vintage released as the estate's Reserva tier

wines.description: Portia's Reserva-tier Ebeia from Gumiel de Izán, aged 16 months in French oak with concentrated dark cherry, cassis, cedar and firm structure suited to medium-term cellaring.

wines.name: Portia Triennia
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new French oak

taste.summary: Portia's prestige Triennia label from a single selected parcel: concentrated cassis, violet, graphite and cedar from 22 months in new French oak, representing the Gumiel de Izán terroir at its most concentrated and cellar-worthy.

pairings.why: Dense tannins and concentrated black fruit need the fat and complexity of dry-aged prime beef.

pairings.dish: Braised wild boar with olives and herbs
pairings.why: Graphite and violet notes match gamey wild boar with aromatic herbs.

pairings.dish: Aged Manchego DOP with fig jam
pairings.why: Polished tannins and concentrated fruit are balanced by the sweet-savoury cheese combination.

history.summary: Portia Triennia is the prestige single-parcel label from Bodegas Portia, named for the three key elements of the wine: terroir, time and tradition. It is produced from the estate's best old-vine Tinto Fino plots and aged 22 months in new French oak in the Foster + Partners gravity-flow cellar.

milestones.event: First Portia Triennia vintage released as the estate's prestige label

wines.description: Portia's prestige single-parcel Triennia from Gumiel de Izán, aged 22 months in new French oak in the Foster + Partners winery with concentrated cassis, violet, graphite and polished tannins.

wines.name: Emina Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: Classic Crianza from the Matarromera group's Peñafiel estate: cherry, plum and vanilla from 14 months in mixed oak, offering accessible, everyday Ribera from a large-format wine tourism producer.

pairings.why: Red fruit and balanced cedar match lamb's mild flavour and aromatic herb seasoning.

pairings.dish: Roast chicken with garlic and pimenton
pairings.why: The wine's everyday style and smooth tannins complement well-seasoned roast chicken.
Approachable red fruit and moderate tannins suit a Spanish charcuterie-cheese board.

history.summary: Bodegas Emina is the flagship estate of the Matarromera group at Peñafiel, one of the most-visited wine complexes in the appellation. The Crianza has been the estate's volume workhorse since 2002, supporting the large-scale tourism operation that includes a wine museum and hospitality centre.

milestones.event: First Emina Crianza vintage released from Peñafiel estate

wines.description: Classic Crianza from the Matarromera group's Peñafiel estate Emina, with cherry, plum and vanilla from 14 months in mixed oak for accessible everyday Ribera drinking.

wines.name: Emina Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 20 months in French oak

taste.summary: The Emina Reserva applies 20 months in French oak to produce a fuller-bodied, more structured expression: dark cherry, plum jam and tobacco, with firm tannins and mineral finish from the Peñafiel limestone soils.

pairings.why: Firm structure and dark fruit balance the rich Castilian cochinillo.

pairings.dish: Beef casserole with mushrooms and herbs
pairings.why: Tobacco and cedar notes echo the earthy mushrooms and slow-cooked beef.
Firm tannins and cedar complement the aromatic honey-drizzled aged cheese.

history.summary: Emina Reserva extends the French oak ageing to 20 months and draws from the estate's best Peñafiel parcels. The Matarromera group's investment in this estate has produced a Reserva that consistently outperforms its price point.

milestones.event: First Emina Reserva vintage released from selected Peñafiel parcels

wines.description: Structured Reserva from Bodegas Emina at Peñafiel, aged 20 months in French oak with dark cherry, plum jam, tobacco and firm tannins from Peñafiel limestone soils.

wines.name: Emina Atio
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new French oak

taste.summary: Emina's prestige single-vineyard Atio from the best Peñafiel parcels: concentrated cassis, graphite and cedar from 22 months in new French oak, with polished tannins and long potential.

pairings.why: Dense graphite tannins and concentration require the fat and char of prime beef.
Cassis and graphite mirror the gamey venison and dark fruit reduction.

pairings.dish: Manchego DOP viejo with black truffle
pairings.why: Polished tannins and concentrated fruit pair with aged cheese and earthy truffle.

history.summary: Emina Atio is the prestige single-vineyard label from Bodegas Emina at Peñafiel, produced from the estate's best old-vine Tinto Fino parcel and aged 22 months in new French oak. It represents the Matarromera group's most ambitious Ribera expression.

milestones.event: First Emina Atio vintage released as the estate's prestige single-vineyard label

wines.description: Emina's prestige single-vineyard Atio from the best Peñafiel old-vine parcels, aged 22 months in new French oak with concentrated cassis, graphite and polished tannins.

wines.name: Museum Real Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: Classic Crianza from the underground-cellar museum estate near Peñafiel: red cherry, plum and vanilla from 14 months in mixed oak, accessible and well-balanced from the Valladolid limestone terroir.

pairings.why: Classic Ribera pairing -- the wine's red fruit and cedar complement herb-crusted lamb.
Smooth tannins and cherry fruit match the clean flavour of the pork chop.
Balanced everyday red pairs comfortably with medium-aged sheep's cheese.

history.summary: Finca Museum is named for the underground cellar museum beneath the Peñafiel estate that displays historic winemaking equipment. The Museum Real Crianza is the estate's classic Ribera label, produced from Tinto Fino on the Valladolid limestone plateau.

milestones.event: First Museum Real Crianza vintage from the Peñafiel estate

wines.description: Classic Tinto Fino Crianza from Finca Museum near Peñafiel, aged 14 months in mixed oak with red cherry, plum and vanilla in an accessible, well-balanced style.

wines.name: Museum Real Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 20 months in French oak

taste.summary: Museum's Reserva tier adds weight and structure: concentrated dark cherry, cassis and tobacco from 20 months in French oak, with firm tannins and a mineral thread from the Peñafiel limestone soils.

pairings.why: Firm structure and concentrated dark fruit balance the Castilian cochinillo.
Dense tannins and dark fruit suit the richness and fat of the beef-bone combination.
The wine's mineral thread and concentrated fruit pair with nutty aged cheese.

history.summary: Museum Real Reserva draws from the estate's best Tinto Fino selections, spending 20 months in French oak barrels to develop concentration and structure. It represents the Finca Museum cellar museum estate's ambition beyond the everyday Crianza tier.

milestones.event: First Museum Real Reserva vintage released from Peñafiel estate

wines.description: Structured Reserva from Finca Museum near Peñafiel, aged 20 months in French oak with concentrated dark cherry, cassis, tobacco and firm tannins from the Valladolid limestone terroir.

wines.name: Ribera del Pelayo Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French oak

taste.summary: Terroir-focused Crianza from the small Hoyales de Roa estate on the Roa de Duero plateau, with cherry, plum and cedar from 12 months in French oak in a restrained, mineral style typical of the Burgos chalk-limestone.

pairings.dish: Roast chicken with herbs and garlic
pairings.why: Medium body and red fruit match the mild, herb-fragrant roast bird.
Classic Ribera pairing -- the wine's cherry and cedar complement the mild lamb.

pairings.dish: Cured jamon iberico with tomato bread
pairings.why: Fresh red fruit and balanced acidity complement the saline fat of iberico ham.

history.summary: Bodegas Ribera del Pelayo occupies a small estate in Hoyales de Roa on the limestone plateau of the Roa de Duero sub-zone in Burgos province. The Crianza reflects the estate's focus on terroir expression from old Tinto Fino vines in this less commercially prominent corner of the appellation.

milestones.event: First Ribera del Pelayo Crianza from Hoyales de Roa old-vine estate

wines.description: Terroir-focused Crianza from the small Hoyales de Roa estate Bodegas Ribera del Pelayo, aged 12 months in French oak with cherry, plum and cedar in a restrained, mineral style.

wines.name: Ribera del Pelayo Reserva
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak

taste.summary: The Reserva from Bodegas Ribera del Pelayo reveals the depth of the Hoyales de Roa old-vine terroir: dark cherry, blackberry and tobacco from 18 months in French oak with firm, age-worthy tannins and chalk-limestone mineral thread.

pairings.why: Concentrated dark fruit and firm structure are natural companions for slow lechazo.
Firm tannins and dark fruit suit the marbled Iberico presa with aromatic seasoning.
The wine's mineral thread and tobacco notes pair with saline aged cheese and olives.

history.summary: The Ribera del Pelayo Reserva is produced from the estate's oldest vines at Hoyales de Roa and benefits from 18 months in French oak. The Roa de Duero plateau's chalk-limestone subsoil provides the mineral backbone that distinguishes these wines from warmer-sector Ribera.

milestones.event: First Ribera del Pelayo Reserva from Hoyales de Roa old vines

wines.description: Old-vine Reserva from Bodegas Ribera del Pelayo at Hoyales de Roa, aged 18 months in French oak with dark cherry, tobacco, cedar and a chalk-limestone mineral backbone.
